The 5711/1A-018 features a brass dial with Tiffany Blue lacquer and with horizontal Nautilus embossing, black printed Patek Philippe Genève, and Tiffany & Co. signatures (hence the term double-signed Nautilus), applied hour markers in blackened 18k white gold, a touch of luminous paint and baton-style hands. Hidden under that trademark blue dial is the Patek Philippe Caliber 26-330 S C, a self-winding movement with a power reserve between 35 and 45 hours – lagging considerably behind more advanced movements of the day engineered to provide between 60 and 72 hours of power reserve. Still, the movement is impressively thin, cramming 212 components into a space of 27mm by 3.3mm. This feat allows for the Nautilus case to take on its signature slimness, measuring in at just 8.3mm-thick.

The 42mm Patek Philippe Annual Calendar ref. 5905R-001 presents a subtle choreography of complications within a warm rose gold frame. Its deep brown dial features luminous rose gold hands that trace the passage of time over an annual calendar and flyback chronograph. Function and elegance intertwine in this Patek piece. It is powered by the automatic caliber CH 28‑520 QA 24H, delivering approximately 55 hours of power reserve.

This pre-owned Patek Philippe Annual Calendar Brown Dial 18K Rose Gold Watch 5905R-001 is accompanied by papers and the manufacturer's box.

Every watch sold by us is thoroughly authenticated and inspected by in-house specialists. We guarantee 100% authenticity and precise condition descriptions on all listings.

SHIPPING & HANDLING:
All orders follow a strict packaging procedure — every watch is carefully packed, fully protected, and 100% insured throughout transit.

U.S. Domestic:
All shipments are sent via FedEx Priority Overnight, fully insured. We only ship to closest FedEx Ship Center (Hold for Pickup) to the address or to a verified business address
— We do not ship to residential addresses.

FedEx Hold-for-Pickup Procedure:
• Package is addressed to the buyer’s name (one additional authorized name may be added upon request)
• A government-issued photo ID and tracking number are required to collect the package
• FedEx will release the package only to the registered pickup name

We do not ship on Saturday or Sunday.

International Shipping (If Applicable):
If you are outside the U.S., please message before purchasing to confirm destination eligibility.
• Buyer is responsible for all import duties, taxes, and customs fees
• Refused or returned international shipments may incur restocking fees.

PAYMENT:
Payments are accepted via Chrono24 Trusted Checkout, providing full buyer protection.
Funds are held securely by Chrono24 until the buyer confirms receipt and satisfaction.

Pricing

Our prices reflect the competitive value of new and unworn watches, complete with all accessories and a valid manufacturer’s warranty. While we do accept offers to close deals, please understand that our prices are already highly competitive in the market.

Inspection

Before shipping, every watch undergoes a thorough inspection to ensure it matches the description in the listing. We check the timekeeping, case, bracelet, and overall condition, ensuring it’s in perfect order. Each watch is securely packed in its box to ensure safe delivery.

Returns

Buyers have two weeks to inspect their purchase. If the watch is not as described or does not function properly, you may return it for a full refund. Returns will only be accepted if the watch is in the same condition as it was shipped, including all original paperwork and accessories. Watches that have been worn, used, or altered in any way will not be eligible for a return.

Taxes

Sales tax is calculated and added at checkout based on your shipping address, in accordance with state and local laws. Chrono24 collects and remits these taxes directly, and we do not have the ability to waive or alter this amount.

Shipping & Delivery

• United States: All packages are shipped via Overnight shipping and can be delivered to a business address, FedEX office (Hold For Pickup) or a residential address.

• International: We welcome international buyers, and duties/customs fees are the buyer’s responsibility. Certain countries may have shipping restrictions due to insurance limitations, so please contact us to confirm availability.

Why Choose Us?

• Over 24 years of expertise and countless satisfied customers worldwide.
• A commitment to authenticity, transparency, and customer satisfaction.
• Competitive pricing, a 2-year warranty, and secure worldwide shipping.
• Chrono24’s escrow system ensures your funds are secure until your watch is delivered and confirmed.

We look forward to making your next watch purchase seamless and enjoyable. If you have any questions or need assistance, feel free to reach out for a consultation

The Patek Philippe 5711/112P Nautilus represents the zenith of sports watch sophistication. This 40mm platinum masterpiece features a striking ruby bezel that adds vibrant elegance to the iconic integrated bracelet design. The Nautilus case, with its distinctive porthole silhouette, has defined luxury sports watches for generations and remains instantly recognizable among connoisseurs. Crafted entirely in platinum, this timepiece offers exceptional durability and prestige while maintaining the refined proportions that make the Nautilus legendary. The ruby accents provide a bold aesthetic statement without compromising the watch's understated elegance. A pinnacle acquisition for collectors who appreciate both technical excellence and timeless design heritage.

Patek Philippe expands its range of elegant ladies' watches with a new version of the self-winding Calatrava Reference 4997 in rose gold decked out in purple – an original and refined color that perfectly matches the warm nuances of the precious metal and the diamond-set bezel.
The dial base embossed with a concentric waves pattern is coated with more than fifty successive layers of translucent lacquer creating a fascinating depth effect. The shade of the dial is echoed by the purple calfskin strap with satinated finish and rose gold prong buckle.
The case houses the ultra-thin self-winding Caliber 240.

Launched in 1996, the patented annual calendar has been a success for the last 20 years. One of Patek Philippe's flagship models, this clever mechanism indicates the day of the week, the date, and the month and only needs to be corrected once a year. It provides a variety of displays and subtleties of style, consistently fusing exquisite lines, high-end materials, and lavish finishing touches.
Hour markers are applied in gold on silvery opaline. rose gold. Diameter of case: 38.5 mm. 11.2 mm in height. waterproof up to 30 meters. Return of the sapphire crystal case. Alligator, shiny chocolate brown. clasp that folds over. Calendar for the year. moon phases and a 24-hour clock. The seconds hand should be swept. The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711/1A-014 is one of the most coveted modern luxury sports watches, representing the final evolution of the iconic steel Nautilus. It retains the classic Gérald Genta–inspired design while introducing a distinctive and elegant olive green dial that sets this reference apart.

Housed in a 40 mm stainless steel case with the signature porthole-shaped bezel, the watch offers a slim profile at approximately 8.3 mm thick, making it exceptionally comfortable and versatile. The integrated stainless steel bracelet flows seamlessly from the case, combining sporty strength with refined finishing.

The dial features a sunburst olive green finish with the Nautilus’ hallmark horizontal embossing. Applied white gold hour markers and luminous hands ensure excellent legibility, while a discreet date window at 3 o’clock maintains balance and practicality.

Powering the watch is Patek Philippe’s in-house Caliber 26-330 S C, an automatic movement known for its precision, reliability, and refined finishing. It offers a power reserve of approximately 45 hours, hacking seconds, and a smooth central seconds hand. The sapphire caseback allows a clear view of the movement’s craftsmanship.

The Patek Philippe Reference 5004P stands as a crowning achievement in modern horology, introduced in the late 1990s as the brand’s first serially produced perpetual calendar with a split‑seconds chronograph. Crafted in platinum, this model houses the legendary Lemania‑based caliber CHR 27‑70 Q, a manually wound movement celebrated for its technical complexity and Geneva Seal finishing. With a 36.5 mm stepped case and a thickness of approximately 15 mm, it bridges the aesthetic lineage of the revered 3970 and 1518 while offering a subtly more robust wrist presence. Since this model is an earlier series and in platinum, the case and lugs are not as "sharp" as later platinum and gold examples, The black dial presentation—rare in this reference—adds a tuxedo‑like elegance to its functional sophistication, including moonphase, leap‑year, 24‑hour, date, day, and month complications. Produced in very limited numbers between approximately 1996 and 2012, the 5004P is among the rarest and most coveted of Patek’s modern grand complications. Its dual chronograph hands allow split‑timing, a feature that elevates its mechanical allure and collector desirability. This platinum variant, especially on alligator strap, is a pinnacle of both technical mastery and understated luxury, a true grail for seasoned connoisseurs. Like New with Patek Philippe travel pouch, setting pin, additional solid caseback, extract confirming sale from 1998 and original certificate dated 1998.

The Patek Philippe Complications 5905R-001 perfectly blends contemporary design with traditional haute horlogerie. Crafted in 18K rose gold, this stunning timepiece unites a self-winding flyback chronograph with an annual calendar, offering both technical mastery and timeless elegance.

The deep brown sunburst dial transitions beautifully to black along the edges, providing exceptional contrast for the applied rose gold hour markers and faceted dauphine hands. Three apertures display the day, date, and month in a clean, legible layout beneath 12 o’clock, while the 60-minute chronograph counter is elegantly positioned at 6 o’clock.

At its heart beats the in-house Caliber CH 28-520 QA 24H, a self-winding movement visible through the sapphire crystal caseback. It features a column wheel with a vertical clutch, ensuring smooth chronograph operation, and a 24-hour indicator that distinguishes day from night. With a 45-hour power reserve, it delivers precision worthy of Patek Philippe’s uncompromising standards.

The watch is paired with a hand-stitched matte brown alligator leather strap and a rose gold folding clasp, ensuring both comfort and sophistication on the wrist.

The Patek Philippe 5905R-001 stands as a symbol of refined excellence — ideal for the collector who values mechanical complexity, understated luxury, and the enduring prestige of one of the world’s greatest watchmakers.

A ref. 2526R Calatrava with double signed Freccero first series enamel dial. During production of the 2526 in the 1950s enamel dials were standard practice but Patek now reserves the process only for special editions. The Freccero signature, a prominent Uruguayan retailer, adds additional context to this example’s personal history. It features Patek’s first ever automatic calibre, 12-600 AT, and potentially the first movement with engraving on its 18k rotor. Its technical significance was so great that at the time of its release, the list price was comparable to that of a 2499. The 2526 is classic Calatrava aesthetics with a movement many still consider to be the greatest ever time-only calibre.

Worth Reading:

The Reference 2526, here in pink gold, is a classic 1950s enamel-dial Calatrava that introduced major advances in Patek Philippe’s movement technology. Its calibre 12-600 AT was the brand’s first automatic movement and an early use of Patek’s now-iconic Gyromax balance wheel. Produced from 1953 to 1960 in four case metals, the 36mm 2526 features a baked enamel dial, this example with double “Freccero” signature by the Uruguayan retailer. Its technical significance was so great that the original list price of a 2526J on bracelet was 3,400 CHF, just 400 shy of a 2499J.

The 2526 features an enamel dial, a process Patek now reserves for special editions. Early “Mark 1” dials, such as this example, were twice baked: first to form the enamel base with holes for the indices, then indices were inserted and another enamel layer was added and fired, resulting in small dimples around the indices.  Later “Mark 2” dials had indices applied after baking. Only around 1 in 10 dials survived production. The 36mm case is classic Calatrava, and features a double-P crown with guilloché motif, inspiration for today’s Patek Standard Seal.

Before brand boutiques trusted retailers like Freccero, founded in 1868 in Uruguay, were the premier location for watch sales abroad. Double-signed now command a premium for their rarity and historical context. To some they add additional intrigue, to others connection. The calibre 12-600 AT within the 2526 was Patek’s response after expiration of Rolex’s patent of its full-rotor technology. It features Patek’s trademark Gyromax balance with recessed timing weights rather than adjustment screws, a swan-neck regulator, self-compensating hairspring, and an 18k gold rotor with guilloché finishing, likely the first of its kind. 

For a watch with classic aesthetics, the 2526 offered meaningful technical innovation. On the surface it is a beautiful enamel dial Calatrava, simple and understated time-only perfection from a South American retailer. However, the engineering and watchmaking prowess beneath the caseback was not only ahead of its time, many still consider it to be the greatest time-only movement ever made.

Condition:

This 2526R presents in good overall condition consistent with its age. The mark 1 enamel dial shows no signs of cracks with some light patina, particularly around the indices. The case has signs of restoration and has likely seen a polish though hallmarks are still present. Some light scratches exist on the acrylic crystal and the case. The watch is running within spec. on our timing equipment.

Experience the sophistication of global timekeeping with the Patek Philippe World Time 5130J-001 — a masterpiece that embodies refined elegance, technical precision, and timeless prestige. Crafted in luxurious 18k yellow gold, this highly sought-after reference is part of Patek Philippe’s iconic World Time collection, known for its ingenious ability to display time in 24 time zones simultaneously.

The watch features a radiant silver guilloché dial with a center sunburst pattern, encircled by a city ring and a 24-hour disc, allowing for effortless tracking of global time. The elegant arched hands, a hallmark of this model, sweep gracefully across the dial, enhancing both legibility and charm.

Housed in a 39.5mm case, the 5130J-001 offers a balanced and sophisticated wrist presence, perfect for collectors and travelers alike. Powered by the ultra-thin Caliber 240 HU automatic movement with micro-rotor, this watch exemplifies Patek Philippe’s commitment to horological excellence and technical innovation.

Whether you're crossing time zones or collecting iconic haute horology, the World Time 5130J-001 is a symbol of heritage, complexity, and understated luxury.

Born as the most opulent expression of the Nautilus chronograph, this full rose gold execution elevates Gérald Genta’s iconic porthole design with commanding warmth. A black gradient dial with signature monocounter layout delivers bold symmetry and unmistakable presence.

Presents itself in mint condition with little to no signs of wear. Featuring a black gradient dial with horizontal embossing and rose gold luminous markers, the display offers depth and warmth from every angle. Showcasing brushed and polished 18K rose gold surfaces across the case, the iconic porthole architecture reflects light with sculpted precision. Completing the composition, the integrated rose gold bracelet reinforces its seamless and commanding presence.

Functions include hours, minutes, seconds, date, and flyback chronograph. The screw-down crown on the right side of the case sets the time and quick adjusts the date when pulled to the first position. The top pusher starts and stops the chronograph, while the lower pusher activates the flyback reset. Luminous hands and markers ensure low-light legibility.

Comes as a full set, double-boxed with a watch pillow, Certificate of Origin, instruction manual, accompanying literature, Museum booklet, and leather folio.

Few watches balance sport and opulence with this level of conviction, where rose gold architecture meets purposeful chronograph engineering. In this configuration, the Nautilus becomes more than iconic — it becomes definitive.

This Patek Philippe Nautilus Chronograph Ref. 5980/1R-001 is one of the most prestigious and recognizable luxury sports watches ever produced. Crafted entirely in solid 18K rose gold, this model combines the iconic Gérald Genta Nautilus design with the technical sophistication of a high-end chronograph.

The watch features a rich brown sunburst dial with horizontal embossing, beautifully complementing the warm tones of the rose gold case and bracelet. Inside, the watch is powered by the Patek Philippe Caliber CH 28-520 C automatic flyback chronograph movement, delivering exceptional precision and reliability.

A highly collectible reference, the 5980/1R-001 represents the perfect blend of luxury, sport, and horological craftsmanship, making it a standout piece for serious collectors and enthusiasts alike.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711/1A-014 is a pinnacle of luxury sports watch design, blending elegant aesthetics with technical mastery. Crafted from high-grade stainless steel and measuring 40mm in diameter, this iconic timepiece features a horizontally embossed green dialrich, vibrant, and enhanced by luminous gold-applied hour markers for excellent legibility. The signature porthole-inspired case, integrated bracelet, and satin-brushed finish highlight the Nautiluss instantly recognizable silhouette. Powered by the ultra-thin self-winding Caliber 26-330 S C movement, it offers a date function, stop-seconds capability, and a 45-hour power reserve, all visible through a sapphire crystal caseback. Water-resistant to 120 meters, the 5711/1A-014 is as functional as it is exclusive, making it one of the most coveted stainless steel watches in horological history.

Description

Offered for sale is the timeless Patek Philippe Calatrava reference 6119G-001, one of the most elegant modern interpretations of the legendary Calatrava dress watch.

Crafted in 18k white gold, this reference features the iconic Clous de Paris (hobnail) guilloché bezel, a signature design element historically associated with classic Calatrava references. The watch is paired with a charcoal grey dial with vertical satin finish and applied white-gold hour markers, delivering a perfect balance between traditional Patek elegance and contemporary refinement.

Inside is Patek Philippe’s modern manual-winding caliber 30-255 PS, featuring twin mainspring barrels and approximately 65 hours of power reserve, visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback.

This example is brand new from 2025 and comes as a complete full set, making it an excellent opportunity for collectors seeking a pristine example of one of Patek Philippe’s most iconic dress watches.

General Overview:

In 1968, Patek Philippe introduced the Golden Ellipse, a design that famously broke from the era's standard round or rectangular watches. The watch's name and shape are derived from the Golden Ratio, a mathematical proportion used since antiquity in art and architecture to achieve "divine" harmony. Within the distinctive case shape the Ellipse has began famous also for its recognizable and iconic deep blue color soleil Dial.


Description and Condition Report:

The watch is fitted on a leather strap with original Ellipse 18Kt gold buckle.

PATEK PHILIPPE GOLDEN ELLIPSE – Ref. 3848 – 18k Yellow Gold – Blue Sigma Dial – Box & Papers – 1985 – Very Good
We are pleased to present this elegant and beautifully preserved Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse Ref. 3848, crafted in 18k yellow gold and featuring its iconic blue Sigma dial — a timeless expression of Patek Philippe’s refined design language.
Introduced as part of the Golden Ellipse collection, reference 3848 perfectly embodies the harmony and balance derived from the golden ratio, a principle that has defined this model since its inception. The slim elliptical case in warm yellow gold offers a classic yet unmistakably sophisticated presence on the wrist.
The rich blue Sigma dial, distinguished by the σ σ marks flanking “Swiss,” confirms the use of solid gold dial furniture. Free of numerals and accented by elegant yellow-gold baton hands and applied markers, the dial is a masterclass in restraint and purity, allowing the iconic case shape to take center stage.
Powering this Golden Ellipse is Patek Philippe’s renowned manual caliber 215 movement, prized for its reliability, precision, and exceptionally slim profile — an ideal match for the watch’s elegant proportions.
Dating to 1985, this example is offered in excellent overall condition and is accompanied by its original box and papers, a significant advantage for collectors seeking completeness and authenticity.
Highlights:
Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse Ref. 3848
- 18k yellow gold elliptical case
- Original blue Sigma dial
- High-precision Patek Philippe 215 manual caliber
- Box & original papers dated 1985
- Service letters of Patek from 2009/2019
A classic and highly collectible Golden Ellipse, combining iconic design, precious materials, and period-correct completeness — a quintessential expression of Patek Philippe elegance.
“Few watches capture harmony and timeless luxury as effortlessly as the Golden Ellipse.”

Patek Philippe revisits the sporty style of the Aquanaut chronograph in a rose gold version, paired with a sunburst brown dial featuring a black-gradient rim and matching composite strap.
The original design of the case (water-resistant to 120 m) and the bezel is highlighted by the alternating polished and satin-brushed finishes.
The self-winding flyback chronograph movement combines tradition (column wheel drive) with innovation (vertical disk clutch). Since the clutch causes almost no wear, the central chronograph seconds hand can also be used to display the seconds continuously.
WATCH
Self-winding mechanical movement. Caliber CH 28‑520 C. Flyback chronograph. Central chronograph hand. 60-minute counter. Date in an aperture.
DIAL
Sunburst brown with black-gradient rim, embossed with Aquanaut pattern, gold applied numerals with white luminescent coating.
CASE
Rose gold. Screw-down crown. Sapphire crystal case back. Water-resistant to 120 m. Diameter (10-4 o’clock): 42.2 mm. Height: 11.9 mm.
STRAP
Composite material, dark brown. Aquanaut fold-over clasp.

Often called the Aquanaut “Jumbo”, this white gold reference stands out with its larger 42.2 mm case and deep blue colorway that gives the sporty design added presence and refinement. Produced in small quantities, this timepiece stands as a modern icon within the Aquanaut family.

Presents itself in mint condition with little to no signs of wear. Features a deep navy blue dial revealing a layered depth in changing light, as the embossed pattern draws out highlights that shift from inky blue to a softer maritime tone. Showcasing a jumbo 42.2mm white-gold case that balances polished and satin-brushed finishes, giving the watch a bold yet refined presence that comes alive with movement. Paired with a matching deep navy composite strap, the design is completed with a white-gold fold-over clasp that reassures a sense of craftsmanship and everyday comfort.

Functions include hours, minutes, seconds, and date. Luminous hands and hour markers ensure clear legibility in low-light conditions. Foldover clasp allows for tool-free adjustment of the bracelet length by up to 6mm in either direction, guaranteeing a secure and customized fit during active wear. Self-winding Caliber 330 SC features a quick-setting mechanism for the date and day displays that enables forward adjustment without turning the crown backward or endangering the movement. Screw-down crown preserves water resistance to 120 meters, allowing for dependable everyday wear.

Comes as a full set, double-boxed with watch pillow, warranty paper, leather folio, and manual.

Designed for collectors who value daily connection over display alone, the white-gold Aquanaut offers a sense of substance you can feel and versatility you’ll actually enjoy. The result is a rare modern icon that fits effortlessly into everyday life while still earning its place in a serious collection.

This Patek Philippe Nautilus 5712R-001 is offered in unworn condition and presented as a complete full set, including the original Patek Philippe box, certificate of origin, booklets, and accessories. Crafted in 18k rose gold, the watch is paired with the original brown alligator leather strap and rose gold Nautilus deployant clasp, delivering a refined yet sporty presence on the wrist.

The asymmetrical dial layout features a power reserve indicator, small seconds, pointer date, and moon phase, creating one of the most distinctive and mechanically sophisticated Nautilus configurations. Powering the watch is Patek Philippe’s in-house Calibre 240 PS IRM C LU, an ultra-thin automatic movement with a micro-rotor, celebrated for its elegance, precision, and exceptional finishing.

The 40 mm rose gold case retains crisp factory lines and remains completely unpolished. Sapphire crystals protect both the dial and exhibition caseback, offering durability and a clear view of the movement. A highly sought-after reference combining precious metal, horological complexity, and iconic design, the 5712R-001 represents a standout opportunity for collectors seeking a modern Patek Philippe Nautilus with enduring value and exclusivity.

About

The Patek Philippe Annual Calendar Moon Phase Stainless Steel with a Blue Arabic Dial is a modern masterpiece. This watch highlights Patek Philippe’s dedication to innovation and timeless craftsmanship. It is the first women’s annual calendar crafted entirely in stainless steel. That choice alone makes this model unique within the brand’s history.

The 38mm case blends durability with elegance. Its polished stainless steel bracelet ensures comfort while adding a refined presence. The deep blue dial features a sunburst finish with bold white Arabic numerals. These numerals provide excellent legibility while enhancing its contemporary style.

The annual calendar complication automatically adjusts for months with 30 or 31 days. It only requires a correction once each year, at the end of February. The moon phase display adds a poetic touch, symbolizing Patek Philippe’s artistry and precision. The self-winding Caliber 324 S QA LU movement powers this timepiece. It delivers reliability and accuracy with a 45-hour power reserve.

Collectors admire this model for its rarity and versatility. Stainless steel Patek Philippe watches remain highly sought after by enthusiasts. The combination of technical brilliance and everyday wearability sets it apart.

This piece represents heritage, innovation, and elegance. It is a must-have for collectors seeking both beauty and utility.

A video of this Patek is posted online under title "Patek Philippe Calatrava 3893 Yellow Gold 33mm 1990s".

A true classic, the Patek Philippe Calatrava has embodied enduring style since its inception, evolving subtly through a handful of thoughtful redesigns while maintaining its timeless identity.
This reference 3893 in 18k yellow gold from the 1990s exemplifies that tradition. Its porcelain-like dial—reminiscent of the enamel finishes used in earlier eras—features a creamy ivory backdrop complemented by applied dauphine hour markers and matching hands. A delicate gold dot minute track frames the dial, while the subsidiary seconds register provides balance and symmetry to the overall design.
The case is fitted with a signed Calatrava crown and powered by the manual-wind Caliber 215, a movement known for its slim profile. It is paired with a Patek brown crocodile strap and matching 18k gold tang buckle.
Elegant, and unmistakably refined, this is a piece you simply can’t go wrong with—a cornerstone dress watch that will remain stylish for decades to come.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5712R-001 in 18k rose gold is one of the most elegant and technically refined executions within the Nautilus collection. This reference pairs the iconic case design with a sophisticated grey dial configuration, giving it a more modern and understated presence compared to the brown dial variant.

The 40mm rose gold case features the signature rounded octagonal bezel with alternating satin-brushed and polished finishes that emphasize the sculpted architecture of the Nautilus. The grey horizontally embossed dial displays Patek Philippe’s distinctive asymmetrical layout, including a moon phase, power reserve indicator, date by hand, and small seconds — a design that has become instantly recognizable among collectors.

Inside, the watch is powered by the ultra-thin automatic Caliber 240 PS IRM C LU, featuring a 22k gold micro-rotor and visible through the sapphire caseback. The movement reflects Patek Philippe’s high standards of finishing and mechanical refinement.

This example is a 2022 full set in like-new condition and comes fitted on a brown alligator strap with an 18k rose gold folding clasp, enhancing its elegance while maintaining the sporty DNA of the Nautilus.

A highly collectible and increasingly scarce reference that blends precious metal warmth with one of the most balanced and technically interesting dial layouts in the modern Patek Philippe lineup.

The Patek Philippe Twenty~4 Automatic 7300/1200 is a luxury ladies’ watch from the Patek Philippe Twenty~4 collection, known for combining elegant jewelry styling with a mechanical automatic movement.

Description

The Patek Philippe 7300 is designed as a sophisticated everyday luxury watch for women. It features a 36 mm round case with a diamond-set bezel, giving the watch a refined and jewelry-like appearance while maintaining classic Patek Philippe craftsmanship.

The bezel is set with 160 brilliant-cut diamonds (approximately 0.81 carats) arranged in two rows, adding a distinctive sparkle around the dial. The watch is available in stainless steel or 18k rose gold, usually paired with a fully polished matching bracelet and a fold-over clasp. ￼

The dial is typically a sunburst finish (blue, gray, olive green, brown, or silver depending on the version) with applied gold numerals and luminous hands, offering excellent legibility. A date window at 6 o’clock completes the clean and balanced design. ￼

Inside the watch is the Patek Philippe Caliber 26-330 S C, a self-winding mechanical movement with:
• Sweep seconds
• Date function
• Approximately 35–45 hours power reserve
• 21k gold rotor
• Certified Patek Philippe Seal finishing and accuracy. ￼

Key Specifications
• Model: Patek Philippe Twenty~4 Automatic
• Reference: 7300/1200
• Case Size: 36 mm
• Movement: Automatic Caliber 26-330 S C
• Bezel: 160 factory diamonds (~0.81 ct)
• Functions: Hours, minutes, seconds, date
• Water Resistance: 30 meters
• Bracelet: Integrated steel or rose gold bracelet. ￼

✅ Overall:
The 7300 is one of Patek Philippe’s most popular modern ladies’ mechanical watches, combining high jewelry elements with traditional Swiss watchmaking and everyday wearability.

General Description

The Aquanaut 5165A-001 from 2010 represents one of the most refined mid-size luxury sports watches produced by Patek Philippe. It blends understated elegance with true sport functionality, maintaining the DNA of the original Aquanaut while offering slightly more wrist presence than earlier mid-size models.

Produced for only a few years, it has become a discreet collector’s reference — especially attractive to those who prefer balanced proportions rather than oversized sports watches.

Case

The watch features a 38 mm stainless steel case, shaped in the Aquanaut’s signature rounded-octagonal form inspired by the Nautilus design language.

Slim profile (around 8 mm thick)

Satin-brushed surfaces with polished bevels

Screw-down crown

120 meters water resistance

It wears slightly larger than 38 mm due to the integrated strap and bezel proportions, but still maintains a refined, elegant footprint on the wrist.

Dial

The dial is matte black with the classic embossed “grenade” pattern.

Applied Arabic numerals with white gold surrounds

Luminous hour markers and hands

Central sweeping seconds

Date aperture at 3 o’clock

The texture gives depth without being flashy. In certain lighting, it can appear charcoal or dark grey rather than pure black.

Movement

Inside is the automatic Caliber 324 SC:

Self-winding movement

21k gold rotor

Approximately 35–45 hour power reserve

Hours, minutes, seconds, and date

Visible through the sapphire caseback, the finishing includes Geneva striping, polished bevels, and perlage — clean and refined, though not overly decorative.

Strap & Clasp

Black “Tropical” composite strap

Matching embossed pattern

Stainless steel double-fold deployant clasp

The strap is cut-to-size, which is important for condition and resale considerations.

Position in the Aquanaut Line

The 5165A sits between:

The older 36 mm references

The modern 40 mm 5167A

It offers a middle ground — sporty yet elegant, compact yet modern.

Because it had a shorter production run than the 5167A, it carries a slightly more niche appeal among collectors who favor transitional or less common references.

Introduced in 1997 and produced until 2002, the Ref. 5026 continued Patek Philippe’s Calatrava lineage with a fresh but faithful take on classic design. The small seconds offset at 4 o’clock became a distinctive visual hallmark, setting it apart from more traditional Calatravas while maintaining its understated character.

At the heart of the watch beats the Caliber 240, one of Patek Philippe’s most admired self-winding movements. The micro-rotor architecture allows for an ultra-thin profile while preserving precise chronometry and beautiful finishing, including Geneva striping and anglage.

Available in yellow, rose, white gold, and platinum, the Ref. 5026 stands as one of the most balanced modern Calatravas, offering a perfect midpoint between vintage and contemporary.

The Ref. 5026J captures why Patek Philippe remains the benchmark for dress watches. The Breguet numerals and warm yellow gold case lend it classic charm, while the offset seconds sub-dial introduces just enough asymmetry to make it feel distinct. Combined with the Caliber 240 micro-rotor, it wears slim. A classic Calatrava that rewards close attention.

The watch comes with all original boxes, warranty papers, booklets and two straps - one gray, one black. It is missing the pusher. Warranty papers are dated from May 2016. It is in fair condition with noticeable wear throughout.

The Patek Philippe 5146G-001 is a 39mm white gold annual calendar featuring a warm cream-colored lacquer dial with ivory undertones, often paired with a brown alligator strap. It features Arabic numerals (3, 9, 12), luminous white gold hands, a power reserve indicator (12 o'clock), moon phases, and a 3-year, 31-day annual calendar (6 o'clock).

Key Features and Description:

Dial Aesthetics: The dial is a creamy white or ivory lacquer, creating a vintage, warm, and sophisticated appearance. It includes a white gold, polished, and faceted minute track.

Case and Size: The 39mm 18K white gold case is known for its smooth, rounded design with a 30-meter water resistance.

Complications: The watch includes an annual calendar (days, months, date) that requires manual correction only once a year. It also features a moon phase indicator and a 45-hour power reserve display.

Movement: Powered by the automatic Caliber 324 S IRM QA LU, visible through a sapphire crystal case back.

Markers and Hands: The dial features applied white gold index markers and Arabic numerals at 3, 9, and 12 o'clock.

Band: Typically paired with a matte dark brown, hand-stitched alligator strap with a white gold deployant clasp.

The 5146G-001 is often considered a versatile, modern classic that combines high functionality with elegant, understated design.

NAMED ONE OF WATCHPRO MAGAZINE’S “HOT 100” - THE TOP 100 INFLUENCERS OF THE U.S. WATCH INDUSTRY. HOW CAN WE SERVE YOU?

1940's PATEK PHILIPPE Vintage Mens Square Watch with Dog Ear Lugs - 18K Gold

Very Fine & Beautiful - Art Deco Design - 1 Year Warranty

We are proud to offer the timelessly classic Patek Philippe wristwatch showcased in today's offering. It features an elegant softly curved square case with fabulous dog ear lugs and silver dial with gold accents.

As fine on the inside as the outside, it is powered by an illustrious Patek Philippe 10 ligne movement, unquestionably among the finest hand-winding movements ever built. It possesses all the necessary capabilities to earn it its place as one the finest timepieces in the world.

Extremely high grade, it has swan's neck regulations. Though not marked on the movement it likely originally had eight adjustments - five for chronometer positions and three for heat, cold, and even isochronism. Isochronism is the amount of wind left in the movement. Most watches slow down slightly as their power reserve moves toward empty.

We were fortunate to aquire it with all of its original parts down to the hands and winding crown. It presents pleasingly.

Its 18K gold case has distinctive dog ear lugs in a late Art Deco style. Its egg shell dial with gold accents beautifully complements the case.

Its case presents in excellent condition with only fairly signs of polishing. Its hallmark is still strong. On the wrist it presents in beautiful condition.

Dial: Brushed silver with applied gold baton indices with Roman numerals for the primary hours, subsidiary seconds dial, restored in vintage times, still retains original raised enamel lettering

The markers have a light patina. Please inquire if you would like them cleaned or if you would like a fresh restoration.

Hands: Original, gold "batons"

Movement type: Period, correctly unsigned (this watch was made before it became Patek's customary practice to sign all of their crowns)

Jewels: 18 rubies

Band: Crocodile, remborde, brand new and unworn

We also stock lizard and a variety of other straps in various prints, skins, and colors. Please ask us if you're interested.

Serviced: One of our Master Watchmakers, who used to be the watchmaker for an authorized Rolex dealer and was given training by Rolex at their Beverly Hills Service Center to be an authorized Rolex watchmaker and who has done work for the Cartier Service Center, has fully serviced the watch which consists of fully disassembling the movement, carefully inspecting each part, installing a correct new mainspring, reassembling and lubricating the movement, and extensive timing testing and regulation (an $895 value). 

Timing: Timed on a state-of-the-art computerized system. It is running extremely strongly and keeping very accurate time.

The Blue Rattrapante: Ref. 5370P-011

The Patek Philippe 5370P-011 is widely considered an "end game" watch for the chronograph collector. It is one of the few timepieces that combines a pure manual-winding split-seconds mechanism with the artistic rarity of a Grand Feu enamel dial.

The dial color is mesmerizing—a rich, glossy blue that can only be achieved by firing enamel powder at extremely high temperatures (850°C). Many dials crack during this process, making each perfect example a triumph of craftsmanship. The white gold Breguet numerals float above this deep blue abyss, offering perfect legibility.

Mechanically, the Caliber CHR 29-535 PS is a spectacle. Visible through the sapphire back, the split-seconds clamp and dual column wheels display Patek Philippe’s mastery of traditional horology. Encased in heavy platinum with concave bezels, this is a watch of immense gravity and beauty.

As far as brand identity goes, Patek Philippe, unlike many brands, is seemingly free of being associated with any one model in particular, and instead enjoys a reputation for quality, craftsmanship, and a long-standing pedigree as a top Swiss manufacturer.

Of course, there are models and model families that are nothing short of icons (e.g., Nautilus, Calatrava, Grand Complications, etc.) but if you dig deep enough there are hidden gems that do not even fall into any of these families.

Take the rectangular dress watch that we have here for example. Dating to circa the early 1950s, this piece is reminiscent of what would later become the 'Gondolo,' but predates the model family.

The solid yellow gold case is beautifully-sized, coming in at 28mm x 35mm and shows normal signs of wear throughout with clear hallmarks. Inside, the silver dial features applied indices, a matching 'pencil' handset, and a subsidiary seconds register at the 6 o'clock position. It is powered by one of Patek's manual-winding movements.

Last but not least, this piece is further elevated by an additional retailer signature on the lower half of the dial that reads "Gübelin." How about that!

Lesser-known the model lines like the Calatrava, complicated chronographs, and the Ellipse, Patek's from the earlier part of the twentieth century often predate reference numbers all together and offer surprisingly good value relative to their more established successors. This piece is one of the finest we have had the pleasure of offering on the website, full stop.

...And this piece, with its documentation and extra spice on the dial, makes for something genuinely rare.

The Apex of Sport-Luxury: Nautilus Perpetual Calendar 5740/1G

The Patek Philippe 5740/1G-001 is widely considered the "End Game" Nautilus. It represents the perfect marriage of Gerald Genta’s legendary case design with the high horology that defines Patek Philippe.

What makes this watch truly exceptional is its wearability. Powered by the legendary Caliber 240 Q micro-rotor movement, the case measures a mere 8.42mm in thickness, allowing the heavy 18k white gold bracelet to drape effortlessly on the wrist. It is thinner than many time-only watches, yet it tracks the day, date, month, leap year, and moon phases with mechanical perfection.

The dial is the classic Nautilus Blue Sunburst, but the sub-dials are recessed and snailed, adding a level of depth and complexity not found on the standard models. It is understated, technical, and undeniably luxurious.

The 'Sculpture' family of watches was launched in 1999 as an alternative to Patek's other pieces offered on an integrated bracelet, the Neptune and the Nautilus. Originally, these were intended to be solid exclusively in the Russian market, but with successful sales figures after launch, Patek chose to offer the models worldwide.

Offered in a variety of sizes, metals, and with multiple movements, the piece that we have here, a Ref. 5091/1A, is perhaps the most versatile of variants, with a white dial, 40mm case diameter, and made of stainless steel. It comes to us is excellent overall condition, with a gently previously polished case and bracelet, an immaculate dial inside with Tritium 'Arabic' indices, a matching handset, and a flawless sapphire crystal. The crown at the 3 o'clock position features blue enamel-fill and a 'guard' in the form of a stainless steel bridge that, when in place, blends into the shape of the case. Inside, Patek's Cal. 315 SC automatic-winding movement beats away.

Like the Neptune from this era, the Sculpture was designed to be a real daily-wearable 'elevated' timepiece. This was true in the 1990s, and is true today. We feel that these are truly undervalued, and with these now 'becoming vintage,' we look forward to seeing how these Tritium dials will age as the years go on.

This Patek Philippe Nautilus 5726A-001 is offered in unworn condition and presented as a complete full set, including the original Patek Philippe box, warranty papers, booklets, and accessories. Crafted in stainless steel, the watch features the iconic Nautilus case design paired with a beautifully balanced dial displaying an annual calendar with day, date, and month apertures, as well as a moon phase and 24-hour indicator.

The 40.5 mm case houses Patek Philippe’s in-house Calibre 324 S QA LU 24H/303 automatic movement, renowned for its precision, reliability, and refined finishing. The annual calendar complication requires adjustment only once per year, adding both practicality and mechanical sophistication. Applied hour markers and hands with luminescent coating ensure excellent legibility, while the sapphire crystal and screw-down crown provide durability and everyday usability.

The stainless steel integrated bracelet is unworn and finished with Patek Philippe’s secure fold-over clasp. A highly sought-after Nautilus reference, this 5726A combines sport-luxury design, advanced complications, and strong collector demand, making it an outstanding addition to any serious collection.

Self-winding
Patek Philippe reinterprets the perpetual calendar with retrograde date hand in a new white gold version exuding an original, contemporary style, featuring a dial in gray metallized sapphire crystal revealing the watch's mechanical heart.

The retrograde date as well as the aperture frames of the day (at 9 o'clock), leap-year cycle (at 12 o'clock) month (at 3 o'clock) and moon phases (at 6 o'clock) are underlined by a gray electroplated outline.

The bezel and the caseback are enhanced by the famous hobnail pattern, one of the finest emblems of Patek Philippe style.

About

The Patek Philippe Annual Calendar Flyback Chronograph Rose Gold with a Blue Dial showcases exceptional Swiss craftsmanship and timeless innovation. This model blends technical mastery with refined aesthetics, making it highly desirable among collectors.

The 42mm case features warm 18k rose gold with a polished, elegant finish. Its sunburst blue dial creates a striking contrast, enhancing both depth and legibility. Applied gold hour markers and luminous hands ensure clear visibility in any light.

This timepiece features Patek Philippe’s renowned annual calendar complication. It automatically adjusts for months with 30 and 31 days. The flyback chronograph allows instant reset and restart with a single push. This function adds precision and convenience for everyday use.

Powering the watch is the in-house Caliber CH 28-520 QA 24H. This self-winding movement delivers reliable performance and exceptional accuracy. A sapphire caseback reveals the intricate finishing and mechanical artistry.

The watch represents modern elegance with technical depth. Its combination of complications and bold design sets it apart. The Patek Philippe Annual Calendar Flyback Chronograph remains a must-have for serious enthusiasts seeking innovation and prestige.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 5726/1A-010, released in 2013, is a sophisticated multi-functional sports watch that blends elegance with practicality. Its 40.50 mm stainless steel case features the iconic porthole-inspired design with integrated lugs, while the crisp white dial offers excellent legibility with applied hour markers and a 24-hour display, moon phase, and annual calendar complications. The watch is fitted with a matching stainless steel bracelet, ensuring a seamless, luxurious look and secure fit for daily wear. Despite the listed 12,000 m water resistance—which is likely a typo—the actual rating is 120 meters, making it suitable for swimming and casual water exposure.

Powered by Patek Philippe’s Caliber 324 S QA LU 24H/303, the 5726/1A-010 delivers exceptional mechanical performance with a 45-hour power reserve and refined finishing visible through the sapphire case back. The combination of the annual calendar, moon phase, and 24-hour indicator makes this Nautilus a versatile companion for both formal and casual occasions. As a highly sought-after model, the 5726/1A-010 represents the pinnacle of Patek Philippe’s ability to merge complex horology with timeless design, appealing to collectors who value both functionality and enduring style.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5712R-001 is a refined and highly collectible version of the iconic Nautilus, crafted in 18k rose gold and paired with the brand’s signature grey dial and asymmetrical complication layout.

The rose-gold case features the classic Nautilus rounded-octagonal bezel and measures approximately 40 mm across, with a slim and elegant profile. It is fitted with a sapphire crystal on both the front and the case back, allowing a clear view of the movement.

The grey dial has a subtle horizontal embossing and is distinguished by its off-center and balanced display. It features applied rose-gold hour markers and matching hands, offering excellent legibility while maintaining a very distinctive aesthetic.

Complications include:

Central hours and minutes

Small seconds sub-dial

Power reserve indicator

Pointer date combined with moon-phase display

Inside is Patek Philippe’s ultra-thin automatic caliber 240 PS IRM C LU, featuring a 22k gold micro-rotor. The movement is known for its refined finishing and slim construction, providing approximately 48 hours of power reserve.

The 5712R is delivered on a brown alligator leather strap with an 18k rose-gold fold-over clasp.

In a 2014 example, the Nautilus 5712R represents one of the most elegant and technically appealing complicated Nautilus models, combining sport-luxury design with true high-horology finishing.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5712/1A-001 is an extraordinary expression of rarity, heritage, and refined Swiss craftsmanship. Crafted from stainless steel and measuring 40mm, this highly coveted timepiece features a striking blue index dial adorned with the prestigious Tiffany & Co. stamp, elevating its exclusivity and collector appeal. The signature rounded octagonal bezel preserves the timeless Nautilus identity, and the integrated stainless steel bracelet offers seamless comfort and unmistakable presence on the wrist. Powered by Patek Philippe’s finely finished automatic movement, the Nautilus 5712 delivers exceptional precision and horological artistry. Combining iconic design, advanced complications, and elite provenance, this Tiffany-stamped Nautilus stands as one of the most desirable modern sports watches in haute horology.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711 is one of the most iconic and sought-after luxury sports watches ever made. Crafted entirely from stainless steel, it perfectly balances elegance, simplicity, and exclusivity — a design that has become a legend in the watch world.

Designed by the legendary Gérald Genta, the Nautilus is instantly recognizable for its rounded octagonal bezel, horizontal embossed dial, and integrated bracelet. The blue dial on the 5711 is particularly stunning, shifting in tone from deep navy to vibrant blue depending on the light, giving it incredible depth and character.

The 40mm slim stainless steel case sits comfortably on the wrist, offering a refined yet sporty look that fits any occasion. Inside beats the Patek Philippe Caliber 324 S C automatic movement, visible through the sapphire caseback. This movement is beautifully finished and features the Patek Philippe Seal, representing the highest standard of craftsmanship and precision.

With 120 meters of water resistance, the Nautilus 5711 is as practical as it is luxurious. The steel bracelet is exceptionally well made — smooth, comfortable, and perfectly integrated into the case, showcasing Patek’s unmatched attention to detail.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711 is more than a watch — it’s a statement of understated luxury and fine craftsmanship. Discontinued and highly collectible, it remains one of the most desirable models in modern watchmaking, appealing to both serious collectors and enthusiasts alike.

About


The Patek Philippe Power Reserve Moon Phases Rose Gold with a Rose Dial exemplifies refined complication and timeless elegance. From the first glance, it reflects Patek Philippe’s mastery of classical design and functional sophistication. Moreover, it stands as a distinguished choice for collectors who appreciate understated luxury.

Patek Philippe crafts the 36mm case from 18k rose gold, offering warm tones and balanced proportions. As a result, the watch sits comfortably while maintaining a formal presence. The rose dial enhances visual harmony and features beautifully integrated power reserve and moon phase displays. In addition, applied gold hour markers and dauphine hands ensure clarity and elegance.

The manual-winding Caliber 240 PS IRM LU powers this exceptional timepiece. Therefore, it delivers ultra-thin construction, refined finishing, and reliable precision. The micro-rotor design preserves the watch’s slim profile. Furthermore, the sapphire caseback reveals exquisite movement decoration.

Historically, Patek Philippe’s power reserve and moon phase combinations highlight technical artistry. Consequently, the Patek Philippe 5055R-001 appeals to enthusiasts seeking horological depth, classic aesthetics, and enduring investment value.

A video of this Patek can be viewed online under title "Patek Philippe Rare Peek-a-boo Hobnail 3086 18k Yellow Gold Bracelet 1958".

A joy to interact with, this piece reads as jewelry at first glance, its striking bracelet concealing a hidden dial that reveals itself only when lifted. Produced in limited numbers for a select clientele, this late-1950s example is crafted in 18k yellow gold and built around a brick-style bracelet manufactured by Gay Frères, long favored for Patek Philippe’s dress bracelet watches. The maker’s mark is stamped beneath the clasp.
Each individual “brick” is embellished with fine hobnail decoration, a texture carried throughout the bracelet and clasp, subtly interrupted by the Patek Calatrava emblem. Lifting the cover reveals a minimal dial with a white background, applied gold dauphine hour markers, and slim hands. The cover stays firmly in place when opened, and the crown is discreetly tucked away to preserve the jewelry-like profile.
Charming and quietly confident, this watch blends mid-century design with understated branding. It wears more robustly than it feels and remains a rare and rewarding find.

Presenting the legendary Nautilus 5712/1A…

Considered by many the ultimate steel sport watch. The iconic “Nautilus” shape all started with a drawing on a napkin in 1976 to resemble the portholes of transatlantic ocean liners, particularly their rounded octagonal windows with visible hinges . The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5712/1A-001 was introduced in 2006 to celebrate the 30th anniversary of the Nautilus collection. It truly is the ultimate fusion of luxury, heritage, and exclusivity. With its iconic Gérald Genta design, ultra-thin micro-rotor movement, and beautifully asymmetrical dial featuring a moon phase, it’s both mechanically brilliant and visually striking.

Now discontinued, its rarity makes it even more desirable—prices will likely soar as collectors scramble to secure one. This isn’t just a watch; it’s an investment, a statement, and a piece of horological history.

If you want one, act fast—before it becomes nearly impossible to own.

Fitted with an integrated stainless steel bracelet with a folding clasp, the Nautilus 5990/1A-011 combines comfort, style, and sporty sophistication.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-011 is the ultimate collector’s watch, merging iconic design, advanced mechanics, and timeless luxury.

Introduced as part of the groundbreaking 5035 series, the Patek Philippe 5035G-017 Annual Calendar exemplifies the brand's spirit of technical innovation and fine classic design. This reference is encased in white gold and showcases a sophisticated gray dial adorned with crisp Roman numeral hour markers. Launched in the late 1990s, the 5035 series marked the world's first wristwatch to feature an annual calendar complication, requiring adjustment only once per year. Powered by the meticulously crafted Caliber 315 automatic movement, this watch offers reliability and refined finishing, with a 21K gold winding rotor visible through the sapphire caseback. Measuring 37mm across, the case preserves the balanced proportions favored by Patek Philippe connoisseurs. The presence of beautifully shaped lugs and elegant 'feuille' hands completes the harmonious design. Known for introducing the annual calendar to the modern market, the 5035G-017 remains a benchmark of contemporary horology, coveted by both collectors and enthusiasts for its historical importance and understated luxury.
Reference 5035 is a historic model as it was the first watch to ever feature an annual calendar complication. Accounting for the different lengths of the months, a watch with an annual calendar complication only needs to be set once a year if continuously powered. Since the introduction of the 5035, Patek Philippe has produced other models with this complication and other brands have introduced it in their watches as well.
Like New with Patek Philippe original box, setting pin, and papers.

A ref. 5370P, the brand's landmark first entirely in-house, three-button split seconds which debuted in 2015, here with a blue grand feu enamel dial. Conceived under Thierry Stern with movement development led by Philip Barat and design overseen by Sandrine Stern, it was built to showcase complete in-house chronograph mastery. Its calibre CHR 29-535 PS combined six patented chronograph innovations with a newly engineered rattrapante mechanism that offered smoother engagement and more stable amplitude than earlier generations. Today, the 5370 stands as one of Patek’s most technically important modern complications and a benchmark for the traditional Genevan high complication.

Worth Reading:

The Patek Philippe ref. 5370 Split Seconds Chronograph, launched in 2015, marked the manufacture’s return to the pure split seconds—a complication it had not produced as a stand-alone, non-monopusher model since the mid 20th century. The project began shortly after Patek introduced its first in-house chronograph movement family (cal. CH 29-535) in 2009. Under Thierry Stern’s direction with movement development led by Philip Barat and case/dial design overseen by Sandrine Stern, the goal was to create a flagship rattrapante that combined pre-war Genevan aesthetics with contemporary mechanical rigor. The 5370 debuted in platinum with a black grand feu enamel dial, Breguet numerals, feuille hands, and sculpted lugs that helped define an instantly recognizable silhouette. In 2020, this rich blue enamel dial followed, also in platinum.

The story of its creation centers on Patek’s desire to demonstrate complete autonomy in chronograph construction. Unlike the split seconds chronographs of the 1990s–2000s, which relied on Nouvelle Lémania ébauches, the 5370 uses the fully in-house rattrapante calibre CHR 29-535 PS. This movement represented a major generational leap, incorporating six patented chronograph innovations—many introduced on the 5170 in 2010—now combined with a split-seconds mechanism that was designed from the ground up rather than grafted onto an existing architecture. Key technical achievements include an optimized rattrapante clamp for reduced drag, improved tooth profiles for smoother engagement, a redesigned column-wheel cap for shock resistance, and a significantly more stable amplitude during split-seconds operation—long a weakness of classic rattrapante architecture.

As with almost all modern high-complication Pateks, the manufacture has never released official production totals. However, based on case-number ranges, retailer data, and auction visibility, collectors generally estimate roughly 70-100 examples of the 5370P-001 (black enamel) produced from 2015–2020 and approximately 60-90 examples of the 5370P-011 (blue enamel) produced from 2020 onward, with total lifetime production likely settling around 150-200 pieces combined (not including the recently announced 5370R). Regardless of the precise number, the 5370 stands as one of the most important modern Patek Philippe complications: a demonstration of technical sovereignty, traditional Genevan finishing at its apex, and a quiet but proud return to the golden age of the pure split seconds.

Condition: Brand New / Unworn
Year: 2025

Description

Offered for sale is the highly sought-after Patek Philippe World Time Date reference 5330G-001, one of the newest and most innovative additions to the brand’s Complications collection.

This reference introduces a world-first complication combining the classic Patek Philippe World Time display with a date synchronized to the local time zone, making it both technically impressive and extremely practical for travelers.

Crafted in 18k white gold, the watch features a distinctive opaline blue-gray dial with a carbon-style pattern, surrounded by the iconic 24-city world-time ring and 24-hour day/night display. The watch displays the current time across all 24 global time zones simultaneously, a complication originally developed by Louis Cottier and perfected by Patek Philippe.

Inside is the Patek Philippe caliber 240 HU C, an ultra-thin self-winding movement with a 22k gold micro-rotor and approximately 38–48 hours of power reserve, visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback.

This example is brand new from 2025 and comes as a complete full set, making it an excellent opportunity to acquire one of Patek Philippe’s most modern interpretations of the legendary World Time complication.

You’re looking at an rare historical, collector’s Tiffany&Co pocket watch with a Patek movement. This custom made watch was produced in the late 1800s, between 1890 and the early 1900s. This would be the time period when Tiffany&Co produced their own watches but the manufacturer for the movement was Philippe Patek. The gears are wolf teeth which was also something Patek did with their watches. The watch is a high end and is triple signed. The watch is a lever set pocket watch. These were often times used on the railroad because of their precision with time keeping. The lever was used to ensure the time was not changed with the turn of the crown accidentally.

This is a one of a kind timepiece and a true collectors item. With being over 100 years old, no enamel loss, approximately 2 carats of diamonds. It has name on the back dust cover, and has been clearly loved and preserved over the many years showing no signs of use or polish.

About

The Patek Philippe Nautilus Stainless Steel with Blue Dial is a modern icon of luxury watchmaking. Introduced in 1976, the Nautilus collection redefined the sports-luxury category with its distinctive porthole-inspired design. Crafted entirely from stainless steel, this model combines durability with refined elegance. Its deep blue dial features a horizontal embossed pattern, offering subtle texture and exceptional legibility. Applied white gold hour markers with luminescent coating ensure clear time reading in all lighting conditions.

The 7118/1A-001 houses the caliber 26-330 S C self-winding movement, renowned for its precision and 45-hour power reserve. The slim, rounded octagonal bezel and integrated stainless steel bracelet create a seamless, ergonomic silhouette. Water-resistant to 60 meters, it balances practicality with luxury appeal.

This Patek Philippe Nautilus is more than a watch—it is a statement of style, heritage, and technical mastery. Its combination of iconic design, high-quality materials, and reliable movement makes it a must-have for collectors and enthusiasts worldwide. A true embodiment of Patek Philippe’s commitment to elegance and innovation

A ref. 5004J, the first production Perpetual Calendar Split Seconds Chronograph, an ultimate conclusion to the development of complicated Lemania architecture. Out of an estimated 1000 examples made across all metals, roughly 300 are thought to be in yellow gold. Reportedly hailed by Philippe Stern as the 'spiritual successor to the 2499', the 5004 is easily amongst the most important and desirable Patek Philippe models ever made. The 5004 embodies a technically refined ability to dare that only Patek Philippe at their very best were capable of.

Worth Reading:

Patek Philippe’s ref. 5004 represents the culmination of a particular lineage of high complication under Philippe Stern. It was the first serially produced perpetual calendar split-seconds chronograph, uniting two of watchmaking’s most historic, but separate complications in one calibre. Beyond sheer mechanical audacity, its importance lies in what it represents: the undisputed pinnacle of Patek Philippe’s Lemania ébauche era (here in yellow gold).

Produced from 1994 until approximately 2012, the 5004 was long believed to be limited to roughly a dozen examples per year. While the timeline is accurate, production was likely somewhat higher, with total output estimated just under 1,000 pieces. This includes roughly 300 each in this yellow gold and platinum, 200 in pink gold, 140 in white gold, between 60 and 100 in steel, and a single titanium example. In any metal, the 5004 stands among the most important Patek Philippes ever made.

Philippe Stern reportedly referred to the 5004 as the ‘spiritual successor to the 2499’. While perpetual calendar chronographs already existed, a rattrapante version had never been attempted. The Lemania 2310 (CH 27) was not designed as a split-seconds chronograph, presenting major challenges: an elongated central pinion pushed to its limits and torque drag when activating the split. Patek’s solution was a newly developed isolator mechanism—nicknamed the “octopus” by collectors—which lifts the rattrapante lever to prevent torque on the heart cam, eliminating drag. This innovation, housed within a movement of 407 components, exemplifies the brand’s technical mastery. We also see the utmost of finishing prowess applied here in parallel.

Design-wise, the 37mm case by Guillod recalls the 3970, with stepped lugs, a convex bezel, and an enlarged crown necessitated by the crown-actuated rattrapante. Dials by Stern, Singer, and later Flückiger feature Arabic numerals inspired by the 1518 and early 2499s, pump pushers, and feuille hands throughout. The 5004 is Patek Philippe at its most restrained yet daring—an object of rare balance, vision, and technical refinement.

Condition:

This 5004J is preserved in excellent overall condition. It display only light signs of wear commensurate with occasional use. Its hallmarks are true and deep, the case has never been touched at service and the dial is perfectly preserved. Its calibre CH27-70 Q is running well in spec on our timing equipment. All functions of the calibre CH27-70 Q including time, perpetual calendar, chronograph, and split are operating perfectly.

It comes with full set including box, leather folio, all original documentation, certificate of origin, gold setting pin, and solid caseback, on the pictured Patek Philippe alligator strap with yellow gold deployant clasp.

A ref. 5370P, the brand's landmark first entirely in-house, three-button split seconds which debuted in 2015, here with a blue grand feu enamel dial. Conceived under Thierry Stern with movement development led by Philip Barat and design overseen by Sandrine Stern, it was built to showcase complete in-house chronograph mastery. Its calibre CHR 29-535 PS combined six patented chronograph innovations with a newly engineered rattrapante mechanism that offered smoother engagement and more stable amplitude than earlier generations. Today, the 5370 stands as one of Patek’s most technically important modern complications and a benchmark for the traditional Genevan high complication.

Worth Reading:

The Patek Philippe ref. 5370 Split Seconds Chronograph, launched in 2015, marked the manufacture’s return to the pure split seconds—a complication it had not produced as a stand-alone, non-monopusher model since the mid 20th century. The project began shortly after Patek introduced its first in-house chronograph movement family (cal. CH 29-535) in 2009. Under Thierry Stern’s direction with movement development led by Philip Barat and case/dial design overseen by Sandrine Stern, the goal was to create a flagship rattrapante that combined pre-war Genevan aesthetics with contemporary mechanical rigor. The 5370 debuted in platinum with a black grand feu enamel dial, Breguet numerals, feuille hands, and sculpted lugs that helped define an instantly recognizable silhouette. In 2020, this rich blue enamel dial followed, also in platinum.

The story of its creation centers on Patek’s desire to demonstrate complete autonomy in chronograph construction. Unlike the split seconds chronographs of the 1990s–2000s, which relied on Nouvelle Lémania ébauches, the 5370 uses the fully in-house rattrapante calibre CHR 29-535 PS. This movement represented a major generational leap, incorporating six patented chronograph innovations—many introduced on the 5170 in 2010—now combined with a split-seconds mechanism that was designed from the ground up rather than grafted onto an existing architecture. Key technical achievements include an optimized rattrapante clamp for reduced drag, improved tooth profiles for smoother engagement, a redesigned column-wheel cap for shock resistance, and a significantly more stable amplitude during split-seconds operation—long a weakness of classic rattrapante architecture.

As with almost all modern high-complication Pateks, the manufacture has never released official production totals. However, based on case-number ranges, retailer data, and auction visibility, collectors generally estimate roughly 70-100 examples of the 5370P-001 (black enamel) produced from 2015–2020 and approximately 60-90 examples of the 5370P-011 (blue enamel) produced from 2020 onward, with total lifetime production likely settling around 150-200 pieces combined (not including the recently announced 5370R). Regardless of the precise number, the 5370 stands as one of the most important modern Patek Philippe complications: a demonstration of technical sovereignty, traditional Genevan finishing at its apex, and a quiet but proud return to the golden age of the pure split seconds.

Patek Philippe World Time Date 5330G-001 – White Gold – 2025 – Brand New – Full Set

Condition: Brand New / Unworn
Year: 2025
Scope of Delivery: Full Set (Box & Papers)

Description

Offered for sale is the highly sought-after Patek Philippe World Time Date reference 5330G-001, one of the newest and most innovative additions to the brand’s Complications collection.

This reference introduces a world-first complication combining the classic Patek Philippe World Time display with a date synchronized to the local time zone, making it both technically impressive and extremely practical for travelers.

Crafted in 18k white gold, the watch features a distinctive opaline blue-gray dial with a carbon-style pattern, surrounded by the iconic 24-city world-time ring and 24-hour day/night display. The watch displays the current time across all 24 global time zones simultaneously, a complication originally developed by Louis Cottier and perfected by Patek Philippe.

Inside is the Patek Philippe caliber 240 HU C, an ultra-thin self-winding movement with a 22k gold micro-rotor and approximately 38–48 hours of power reserve, visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback.

This example is brand new from 2025 and comes as a complete full set, making it an excellent opportunity to acquire one of Patek Philippe’s most modern interpretations of the legendary World Time complication.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5980/1AR is a highly coveted luxury sports watch from one of the most prestigious Swiss watchmakers. It combines the iconic design of the Nautilus collection with a chronograph complication, offering both elegance and functionality. Here's a breakdown of its key features and attributes:

1. Case and Design
Material: The 5980/1AR features a rose gold case with a polished and brushed finish. This gives the watch a distinctive warmth and luxury that contrasts with the more common stainless steel versions of the Nautilus.
Diameter: The case measures 40.5mm in diameter, which is a well-proportioned size for a sporty yet elegant watch.
Thickness: The case has a thickness of 12.2mm, which is relatively thin for a chronograph, enhancing the overall sleek look of the watch.
Caseback: The caseback is transparent, allowing a view of the finely decorated caliber 28-520 C movement inside.

2. Dial
The dial of the 5980/1AR is sunburst blue, which transitions in tone depending on the light, giving the watch a dynamic, rich look. It has a horizontal embossed pattern that is characteristic of the Nautilus design.
The chronograph subdials are positioned at 6 o'clock and 9 o'clock, with a date display at 3 o'clock, which is set within a subtle aperture. The date window is small but clear.
The central hands are luminescent, providing better legibility in low-light conditions.

3. Movement
The watch is powered by the Patek Philippe Caliber 28-520 C, an automatic chronograph movement with a column-wheel chronograph and a vertical clutch. This ensures smooth operation of the chronograph functions, with no jumping or stuttering of the chronograph hand.
The movement has a power reserve of 55 hours and operates at a frequency of 28,800 vibrations per hour (4 Hz).
The movement is a work of fine craftsmanship, visible through the transparent caseback, decorated with Côtes de Genève, perlage, and circular graining, reflecting Patek Philippe's high standards of finishing.

4. Bracelet
The 5980/1AR comes with a rose gold bracelet, which features the signature integrated design typical of the Nautilus collection. The bracelet links are brushed on the top and polished on the sides, creating a pleasing contrast.
The bracelet has a folding clasp for added security and comfort.

5. Water Resistance
The watch offers a water resistance of 120 meters, making it suitable for swimming or snorkeling but not deep diving.

6. Complications
Chronograph Function: The watch includes a full chronograph with a flyback function, allowing it to start, stop, and reset with ease.
Date Function: The date is displayed at 3 o'clock.
Small Seconds: The small seconds subdial at 6 o'clock adds to the overall symmetry of the watch while also providing functionality.

7. Style and Prestige
The Nautilus collection, designed by Gérald Genta, is one of the most iconic and sought-after luxury sports watch lines in the world. The 5980/1AR, with its rose gold case and chronograph complication, stands out as a statement piece that blends sporty features with high-end elegance.

First-generation Patek Philippe Nautilus Ref. 5711/1A-001, produced in 2007, and preserved in outstanding, fully original condition. This example is accompanied by its original certificate, presentation box, and full set of brochures, and was serviced by Patek Philippe in May 2025, remaining under factory service warranty.

The present watch belongs to the earliest production of the 5711, distinguished by its highly sought-after Mark I dial, identifiable by its subtle graphic traits and proportions that enthusiasts widely regard as the most faithful modern interpretation of the original Ref. 3700 “Jumbo.” The visual balance of the dial—combined with the thinner profile—gives the watch a notably purer, more restrained presence than later executions.

Powered by the Caliber 315 SC, this first-generation 5711 benefits from a slightly slimmer case profile than subsequent versions fitted with the 324 SC, further reinforcing its closer kinship to the original Nautilus design language.

Importantly, this reference predates Patek Philippe’s transition to the proprietary Patek Philippe Seal in 2011 and therefore bears the Geneva Hallmark, a detail increasingly prized by collectors as a mark of traditional Genevan watchmaking standards and historical continuity.

Production of the 5711/1A-001 was limited to two years, with estimates suggesting fewer than 1,000 examples were made before design revisions were introduced—underscoring the rarity of early, untouched specimens such as the present watch.
The case remains unpolished, retaining crisp lines and original factory finishes. The movement has been professionally serviced by Patek Philippe, while all visible components of the watch remain 100% original.

With the 50th anniversary of the Nautilus approaching, early examples of the 5711—particularly first-generation pieces with Mark I dials, Geneva Hallmark movements, and complete provenance—are increasingly recognized as cornerstone collectibles within the Nautilus lineage. This is a refined and historically significant example of one of Patek Philippe’s most important modern references.

Often appreciated for its wearable dimensions and classic aesthetic, the 3796J stands as a bridge between mid-century ideals and modern sensibilities. Among the most significant perpetual calendar wristwatches of the modern era, the reference 3940 marked Patek Philippe’s return to serially produced ultra-thin perpetual calendars and defined the manufacture’s aesthetic language for decades to follow. Introduced in the mid-1980s and initially produced only in yellow gold, platinum examples appeared only later and in far smaller numbers. A specimen from 1992 represents an early and desirable example of platinum production.

At its heart is the revolutionary self-winding caliber 240 Q, whose ingenious off-center 22K gold micro-rotor allowed the perpetual calendar mechanism to be housed within an exceptionally thin movement. This elegant technical solution made possible the refined proportions that would become a defining characteristic of the reference.

Preserved in excellent condition, the case retains crisp edges and sharply defined hallmarks to the flank—details that suggest minimal polishing and careful ownership. Rarely encountered in such honest state of preservation, this early platinum reference 3940P represents an important and increasingly collectible expression of Patek Philippe’s modern perpetual calendar.

The watch is offered with Extract from the Archives, presentation box, and original platinum buckle.

This Patek Philippe Nautilus 5726A-001 is offered in unworn condition and presented as a complete full set, including the original Patek Philippe box, warranty papers, booklets, and accessories. Crafted in stainless steel, the watch features the iconic Nautilus case design paired with a beautifully balanced dial displaying an annual calendar with day, date, and month apertures, as well as a moon phase and 24-hour indicator.

The 40.5 mm case houses Patek Philippe’s in-house Calibre 324 S QA LU 24H/303 automatic movement, renowned for its precision, reliability, and refined finishing.
