Applied hour markers and hands with luminescent coating ensure excellent legibility, while the sapphire crystal and screw-down crown provide durability and everyday usability.

The stainless steel integrated bracelet is unworn and finished with Patek Philippe’s secure fold-over clasp. A highly sought-after Nautilus reference, this 5726A combines sport-luxury design, advanced complications, and strong collector demand, making it an outstanding addition to any serious collection.

The Patek Philippe Calatrava 5224R-001 is a modern travel watch that combines classic Calatrava elegance with an innovative 24-hour dual-time display, making it both practical and distinctive within the Patek Philippe Complications collection.

Description:
The Patek Philippe 5224R features a 42 mm polished 18k rose gold case with elegant curved two-tier lugs and a slim profile that gives the watch a refined yet contemporary presence on the wrist. The dial is finished in a rich navy-blue color with multiple textures, including a circular-striated center and satin-finished hour ring. Applied rose-gold Arabic numerals and markers are filled with luminescent material for excellent legibility. ￼

One of the most distinctive features of the 5224R is its 24-hour display, which shows both local time and home time on the same dial using two different hour hands. The open-worked hand indicates home time, while the solid syringe-style hand displays local time. A small seconds sub-dial at 6 o’clock completes the layout, creating a balanced and highly legible design. ￼

Inside the watch is the self-winding Patek Philippe Caliber 31-260 PS FUS 24H, featuring a platinum micro-rotor, 48-hour power reserve, and the brand’s Gyromax balance and Spiromax hairspring technology. The movement allows the local time to be adjusted in one-hour increments directly through the crown, making it especially convenient for travelers. ￼

The watch is completed with a navy-blue nubuck calfskin strap and rose-gold pin buckle, maintaining the elegant yet slightly sporty character of this modern Calatrava travel watch. ￼

Key details
• Case: 42 mm 18k rose gold
• Dial: Navy blue with rose-gold applied numerals
• Movement: Automatic Caliber 31-260 PS FUS 24H
• Functions: Dual time zone (Travel Time), 24-hour display, small seconds
• Power reserve: ~48 hours
• Crystal: Sapphire front and caseback
• Water resistance: 30 m

The Patek Philippe Calatrava. Arguably the brands most iconic model line, and purest expression of the brand. Their raison d'etre if you will.

The line has been a hit since its debut in 1932. So much so, that the name "Calatrava" itself has become something of a byword within the watch industry for traditionally styled, no-frills dress watches that simply tell the time. Over the course of its nearly 100-years of production, there have been seemingly countless iterations within the line, with variations in the metal they are made in, with their size, dial configurations, and eventually, complications.

The Calatrava that we have here is a Reference 3435 dating to circa the early 1960s. This example is executed in solid rose gold with a signed rose gold crown, and an automatic-winding movement. The case itself is wonderfully sized at 34mm in diameter and features a 'stepped' design with lugs that turn down nicely for a very svelte feel on the wrist. Inside, the silver dial features applied rose gold 'stick' indices and a '12' marker at the 12 o'clock position, a subsidiary seconds register at the 6 o'clock position, black printed text and minute markers, and a matching rose gold 'pencil' handset.

The Reference 3435 presents excellent value on the Calatrava spectrum, particularly when compared to its predecessor, the Reference 565 and uncommon rose gold examples like the one that we have here.

There are watches, and then there are watches! This is one of the latter.

A ref. 5990/1A-001 Nautilus Travel Time Chronograph. Debuting in 2014, the 5990 incorporates two highly desired complications in one ultimate sporting package. This is one of just a handful of 5990A examples observed with a Tiffany signature subtly within the 6 subdial, reserved for the very best clients of the brand, on the discontinued grey dial: creating something of an apex 5990A.

Worth Reading:

Patek Philippe’s Nautilus ref. 5990 debuted in 2014 to considerable attention, merging two highly desired complications under one reference. A hybrid of utilitarian engineering, the 5990 combined a flyback chronograph and Travel Time for the first time in a Nautilus. A small number of pieces sold through Tiffany & Co.’s 5th Avenue flagship carried a discreet Tiffany signature in the 6 o’clock subdial; this discontinued grey-dial variant is considered the apex 5990A.

Development of the 5990A blended classical Swiss architecture with cutting-edge clutch and integration solutions. Patek’s challenge was fitting two complex systems — a column-wheel flyback chronograph and a Travel Time module — into a slim, robust automatic calibre suited to a sports watch. The answer was the in-house CH 28-520 C FUS, which pairs a traditional column wheel with an advanced vertical (disk) clutch and a Travel Time mechanism derived from Patek patents and historical precedents. Adjusting local time is isolated from the going train so timekeeping isn’t disturbed, and the vertical clutch ensures smooth chronograph engagement with less wear than earlier horizontal designs. Despite its complexity, the movement remains slim, its compactness visible through the sapphire back. Every choice served three priorities: reliability during time-zone changes, intuitive operation, and maintaining Nautilus proportions — all within a 40.5mm case.

Tiffany-signed 5990A examples are exceedingly rare. Auction data confirms at least eight steel watches publicly traded, though private sales and long-held pieces mean the true total is still likely only in the tens. These were reserved for top Tiffany clients, and surviving numbers remain very small.

For collectors or travelers, the 5990 condenses versatility into a single watch. If Genta envisioned the Nautilus as the ultimate sporting luxury design, the 5990 is its fullest expression: a highly resolved, technically advanced reference that compromises nowhere while retaining one of the most recognizable case designs ever created.

Condition:

This 5990/1A-001 presents in great overall condition. The case bears light to moderate signs of surface wear commensurate with occasional use. While there are no significant bashes, signs of surface wear are on most surfaces. That said, the case is unpolished and we know this example's full history definitely in that respect. It comes running well in specification with its original full set. Papers confirm the Tiffany dial and the blue Tiffany outer box survives with its full set down to the bow.

When the retailer matters 

This 1984 Patek Phillippe Ellipse presents the model in one of its most complete and historically meaningful forms. Crafted in 18k yellow gold and paired with its original integrated bracelet, it wears as a unified object rather than a case on a strap, already a rarer expression of the Ellipse before the dial is even considered.

What truly elevates this example is the Hausmann & Co. double-signed dial, a detail reserved for watches retailed through the famed Roman jeweler. Much like Tiffany & Co. in the United States, Hausmann & Co. occupied a privileged position within Patek Philippe’s retail network, supplying a discreet, high-profile European clientele. Double-signed dials from Hausmann were produced in very small numbers and are now regarded as materially more collectible than standard examples.

Why this configuration is so impressive

Based in Rome, Hausmann & Co and long associated with supplying high-profile clientele, diplomats, and Italian aristocracy. Much like Tiffany & Co. in the United States, Hausmann & Co. was selective about what carried its name, and Patek Philippe double-signed examples were produced in very small numbers for their boutique. It tells you where the watch was sold, who it was intended for, and places it firmly within a specific cultural and geographic moment.

Paired with the Ellipse’s integrated 18k yellow gold bracelet, the watch reads as a complete, top-tier execution of the model. The bracelet is fluid, substantial, and integral to the design, reinforcing the Ellipse’s identity as a finished object rather than a case with an attachment.

Correctly Preserved 

The bracelet remains in good condition with solid structure and wear consistent with age. The case shows light marks in line with careful use over time. The blue dial is vibrant, with a visible mark near 11 o'clock that dies not overwhelm the dial or character of the watch. The crystal is clean, and all components are original. Mechanically, the watch is running well for its age. 

An Ellipse with real context

This is not just a beautiful Patek Philippe, it’s a watch with added historical promenance. The combination of the Hausmann & Co. double stamp, integrated gold bracelet, and classic blue Ellipse dial places it a clear step above standard examples.

For someone who values provenance as much as design, and understands why retailer signatures matter, this is a compelling and increasingly difficult-to-find variation. Elegant, historically grounded, and ready for its next chapter.

Patek Philippe Nautilus Ref. 5712/1A-001 'Bespoke Stealth' Black DLC Steel

This Patek Philippe Nautilus Ref. 5712/1A-001 “Bespoke Stealth” represents a striking and highly unconventional interpretation of one of the most revered modern luxury sports watches ever created. Black DLC (Diamond-Like Carbon) coating, giving the watch a dramatic, contemporary presence while preserving the unmistakable Gerald Genta designed Nautilus architecture beneath.

The watch retains the signature asymmetrical Nautilus case, measuring 40mm in diameter (measured across the bezel at 10–4 o’clock, per Patek Philippe’s specifications). The black DLC finish transforms the softly contoured octagonal bezel and integrated bracelet into a sleek, stealth-like silhouette, accentuating the Nautilus’ industrial elegance and architectural depth.

The dial remains faithful to the legendary Nautilus 5712 layout, featuring a horizontally embossed dark-toned surface with contrasting indications. Complications are displayed in a beautifully balanced arrangement:

Power reserve indicator at 10 o’clock
Small seconds subdial at 4 o’clock
Pointer date with moon phase at 7 o’clock

Applied baton hour markers and hands, filled with luminescent material, provide excellent legibility against the dark aesthetic while reinforcing the watch’s sporty yet refined character.

At the heart of this timepiece is the Patek Philippe Caliber 240 PS IRM C LU, an ultra thin, self-winding mechanical movement renowned for its technical sophistication and elegance. The movement features a 22k gold micro-rotor, allowing for a remarkably slim profile while maintaining exceptional efficiency. It operates at 21,600 vibrations per hour and delivers approximately 48 hours of power reserve, with finishing executed to the highest standards of the Patek Philippe Seal.

The integrated bracelet, also finished in black DLC, preserves the Nautilus’ legendary comfort and fluidity on the wrist, with its brushed surfaces and polished accents subtly visible beneath the coating. The folding clasp ensures a secure and seamless fit.

The Patek Philippe Calatrava. Arguably the brands most iconic model line, and purest expression of the brand. Their raison d'etre if you will.

The line has been a hit since its debut in 1932. So much so, that the name "Calatrava" itself has become something of a byword within the watch industry for traditionally styled, no-frills dress watches that simply tell the time. Over the course of its nearly 100-years of production, there have been seemingly countless iterations within the line, with variations in the metal they are made in, with their size, dial configurations, and eventually, complications.

And then, there is this: the Calatrava Reference 2508.

First offered in 1951, it has become one of the more sought-after, collectible, and best-regarded time-only Calatravas ever produced. Why? As you'd expect, there are a few reasons:

The case, which by the way, bears a strong resemblance to the 1463 chronograph, is designed by Taubert and features a 'waterproof' design with a screw-down caseback and comes in at a virtually perfect 35mm in diameter. The dial offerings are all exquisitely crafted, beautifully designed, and features some of the most timeless and stylish applied indices ever. Last but not least, the movement is one of Patek's finest time-only movements ever made, the Calibre 27 SC.

The example that we have here dates to circa 1955 and is crafted out of yellow gold and houses what is known as a 'Series 1A' dial. The overall condition is formidable. All aspects of the case and 'stepped' bezel are beautifully preserved, with clear hallmarks, correct finishing and proportions, and showing just normal wear for its age. The dial inside, thankfully, is in equally well-preserved condition. The desirable applied yellow gold 'stud' and 'dart' indices are complimented by a yellow gold 'dauphine' handset and central seconds hand.

This example comes fitted to a signed brown leather strap with a correct, matching 'PP&Co.'-signed yellow gold buckle by 'AW' and an extract from Patek's archives confirming production in 1955 and its original sale in 1956.

There are watches, and then there are watches! This is one of the latter.

Condition:

Case: Probably very lightly previously polished, retaining symmetrical lugs and correct proportions at all 4 corners with visible factory finishing. The 'stepping' between the bezel and case is clear and present. There at crisp and clear hallmarks visible on the lower right case profile. Shows light signs of wear throughout with no significant blemishes or damage. Later signed crown.

Dial: Stunning and extremely well preserved, with a clean surface showing only one small blemish between the center post and 7:00 marker and excellent 'raised' enamel text. Applied yellow gold 'stud' and quadrant 'dart' indices. Correct yellow gold 'dauphine' handset.

Movement:

Manual-winding Calibre 27 SC by Patek Philippe.

Bracelet/Strap:

Signed brown alligator leather strap with 'PP&Co.'-signed 18k yellow gold buckle by AW.

Patek Philippe Calatrava 'Officers Watch' Reference 5022G-012 in Solid 18kt White Gold with Extremely Rare White Enamel “FD Blanc Porcelaine H-Breguet” Dial, Hand-Painted Black Breguet Arabic Numeral Hour Markers, Black Feuille Hands, Small Seconds at 6 O’Clock, Round Three-Body Case with Curved Lugs and Double Stepped Bezel, Manual-Winding Patek Philippe Caliber 215 PS Movement, Solid Snap Case Back, 33mm.
The 33mm solid 18kt white gold round three-body case, curved lugs, and double stepped bezel are polished. The proportions follow the classic Calatrava architecture, delivering a refined and balanced wrist presence with a slim 7mm profile. The standout feature is the extremely rare white enamel “FD Blanc Porcelaine H-Breguet” dial, executed in traditional enamel with hand-painted black Breguet Arabic numerals. The depth and purity of true enamel create a luminous, glass-like surface that cannot be replicated with lacquer. Black feuille hands complement the dial, and a subsidiary small seconds register is positioned at 6 o’clock for symmetry. The dial, case, and movement are all signed. The watch is fitted with a solid snap case back preserving the purity of the design.

This Patek Philippe 5022G-012 is powered by the mechanical manual-winding Patek Philippe caliber 215 PS movement. Rhodium-plated and finished with fausses côtes embellishment, the movement is constructed with 18 jewels and features a Gyromax balance adjusted for precision. It oscillates at 28,800 vibrations per hour and delivers approximately 44 hours of power reserve. The caliber reflects Patek Philippe’s commitment to compact architecture, fine finishing, and long-term reliability within a slim dress watch format.

First introduced in 1932 with Reference 96, the Calatrava became the benchmark for classic dress watches and established the blueprint for restrained, Bauhaus-inspired design in horology. Reference 5022G continues that lineage with its faithful proportions and traditional enamel dial execution. Though not a limited edition, examples featuring the “FD Blanc Porcelaine H-Breguet” enamel dial are seldom encountered, making this reference particularly desirable among collectors who appreciate traditional craftsmanship and understated refinement. Elegant, historically grounded, and technically precise, this Calatrava embodies the essence of Patek Philippe’s dress watch philosophy.

If you are looking for a classy timekeeper that you don't see everywhere, this is an excellent, ornate option.

For many, owning a Patek Philippe is the ultimate goal of timepiece collecting, and for a certain type, it is the only timepiece they collect. Whichever way you look at it, there is no questioning the elegance of design, the superlative manufacture of their cases and movements, or the sheer elegance and emotion that comes from owning one.

Of course for a brand that is steeped in heritage, we are told “You never actually own a Patek Philippe…”

Well, you know the rest.

A paragon of understated elegance and technical innovation, the present Patek Philippe Ref 565 circa 1952 in rose gold with a double-signed Freccero dial stands as a remarkable collector’s piece—uniting rarity, provenance, and historical significance. Launched in 1938, the Ref 565 (so-called “Etanche”) holds the distinction of being Patek Philippe’s first fully waterproof wristwatch housed in a screw-back case. Unlike many of its contemporaries, which favored aesthetics over function, the 565 epitomized a pioneering blend of utilitarian resilience and timeless design.

The case design is distinctly sportier than its contemporary Calatravas, with the lugs blending flush into the bezel and the movement and screw back case sitting below the lugs. This reference served as the blueprint for the limited-edition Ref 5565A that Patek created to commemorate the reopening of their new Geneva salon.

This example, encased in warm 18k rose gold, exemplifies one of the rarest configurations of the model. Although Reference 565 was predominantly manufactured in stainless steel or yellow gold, specimens crafted in rose gold (with fewer than ten made) are exceedingly rare, especially when found in such remarkable condition. The case, featuring bold, sculptural lugs and a robust 35 mm diameter, was gently polished in the past but it retains great lug proportions and a sharp hallmark under the lugs. The rich patina of this timepiece is testimonial to its age and authenticity.

Further elevating its desirability is the presence of the double-signed dial bearing the signature of Freccero—Patek Philippe’s esteemed retailer in Montevideo, Uruguay. Founded in 1868, Freccero is a renowned family-owned jewelry and watch retailer in South America, known for its lasting partnership with Patek Philippe. Freccero reserved the very few double-signed Pateks for their most valued clients, making them highly prized by collectors. The dial itself is a study in refined minimalism, boasting a beautiful completely original opaline finish, applied gold baton indexes, “dauphine” hands, and subsidiary seconds at six o’clock.

A testament to early waterproof watchmaking, offered here is a supremely rare and captivating reference 565—a jewel of Patek Philippe’s mid-century legacy, imbued with character, provenance, and scarcity. This watch comes with a Patek rose gold buckle, a vintage box, and an Extract of Archives from Patek Philippe dated 2020.

The Patek Philippe 5960/1A-001 is a rare and exceptional timepiece that brings together high horology and modern sportiness in one of the most unique stainless steel models ever produced by Patek.

Crafted from polished stainless steel, the 40.5 mm case gives this complicated watch a refined yet contemporary presence — a striking contrast to Patek’s traditional use of precious metals for their grand complications.

The silver opaline dial is beautifully balanced and easy to read, featuring day, date, and month apertures arranged in an elegant arc beneath the 12 o’clock position. At 6 o’clock, a single monocounter chronograph subdial displays elapsed hours and minutes, creating a clean and symmetrical design. The bold red chronograph hand and subtle blue accents give the dial a touch of modern energy while maintaining Patek’s signature sophistication.

Powered by the in-house Caliber CH 28-520 IRM QA 24H, the movement combines an automatic flyback chronograph with an annual calendar, requiring adjustment only once per year (at the end of February). The beautifully finished movement is visible through the sapphire crystal caseback, showcasing Geneva Seal finishing and a solid 21k gold rotor.

The integrated stainless steel bracelet with polished center links and a folding clasp offers comfort, durability, and an elevated aesthetic that makes this watch perfect for both formal and casual wear.

The Patek Philippe 5960/1A-001 stands out as a true collector’s piece — a rare steel complication that merges sportiness, innovation, and Patek’s signature refinement. It represents one of the most desirable modern references in the brand’s history.

Classic Patek elegance with everyday practicality. The Patek Philippe Annual Calendar 5396R-014 pairs a warm 18k rose gold case with a striking sunburst blue dial that shifts beautifully depending on the light—subtle, refined, and unmistakably high horology. The dial layout is perfectly balanced and highly legible. Patek Philippe World Time Date 5330G-001 – White Gold – 2025 – Brand New – Full Set

Condition: Brand New / Unworn
Year: 2025
Scope of Delivery: Full Set (Box & Papers)

Description

Offered for sale is the highly sought-after Patek Philippe World Time Date reference 5330G-001, one of the newest and most innovative additions to the brand’s Complications collection.

This reference introduces a world-first complication combining the classic Patek Philippe World Time display with a date synchronized to the local time zone, making it both technically impressive and extremely practical for travelers.

Crafted in 18k white gold, the watch features a distinctive opaline blue-gray dial with a carbon-style pattern, surrounded by the iconic 24-city world-time ring and 24-hour day/night display. The watch displays the current time across all 24 global time zones simultaneously, a complication originally developed by Louis Cottier and perfected by Patek Philippe.

Inside is the Patek Philippe caliber 240 HU C, an ultra-thin self-winding movement with a 22k gold micro-rotor and approximately 38–48 hours of power reserve, visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback.

This example is brand new from 2025 and comes as a complete full set, making it an excellent opportunity to acquire one of Patek Philippe’s most modern interpretations of the legendary World Time complication.

The Patek Philippe 5350R-001 Annual Calendar Advanced Research is a compelling expression of horological innovation, introduced in 2007 as a limited edition of just 300 pieces. Housed in a 39 mm 18‑k rose gold case measuring approximately 11.2 mm in thickness, it features the advanced Caliber 324 S IRM QA LU, equipped with a Spiromax balance spring and silicon escape wheel—hallmarks of Patek’s Advanced Research program. The silvery‑gray dial presents a refined annual calendar layout with day, date, month, moonphase, and power‑reserve indicators, all driven by a Geneva‑Seal movement of 36 jewels. The exhibition caseback, engraved “Patek Philippe Advanced Research,” offers a window into the movement’s technical sophistication. Its restrained water resistance of around 25–30 meters underscores its dress‑watch character. As a limited‑edition model that bridges classical complications and cutting‑edge materials science, the 5350R‑001 stands as a highly collectible milestone in Patek Philippe’s modern era. Like New with Patek Philippe Advanced Research box, setting pin and papers dated 2007. Includes Patek Philippe Extract from the Archives dated 2007.

A video of this Patek can be viewed online under title "Patek Philippe Disco Volante 2552 18k Yellow Gold Automatic 1950s".

A highly sought-after ‘Disco Volante’ 2552 from Patek Philippe, manufactured in 1958, defined by its elegantly tapered case and distinctive double-stepped bezel. The silvered dial remains beautifully preserved, featuring a golden dotted minute track, baked enamel print, applied dauphine markers and hands, and a well-proportioned subsidiary seconds register. The crown is embossed ‘PP.’
Its automatic movement contains an 18k gold rotor, prominently stamped ‘Patek Philippe’ and decorated with fine guilloché work. The inner caseback bears the Key #1 hallmark of Wenger, one of Patek’s most respected case makers of the era. It is fitted with a handmade brown crocodile strap and original Patek 18k buckle engraved with the initials A.W. An Extract from the Archives accompanies this rare and distinguished example of mid-century Patek design, offering both mechanical significance and exceptional provenance.

Wear shown and consistent for its age. Hallmarks are visible.
An archive is included.
Case measures 36 x 41 mm, 11 mm thick.
Acrylic crystal.
Serial 7658xx Case 6997xx
Patek caliber 12-600, 30 jewels automatic movement.
Handdn brown crocodile strap.
Patek signed 18k buckle.
18 mm lug width.
Modelled on 6 inch wrist.

A very rare Ref. 1596 (less than 19 known in the market) made in the early 1950s and belonged to Mr. Antonio Lamaro, famous Italian entrepreneur, owner of hotels in Europe and America, and visionary real estate developer, who built the historic five-star Hotel Colón (today the Lamaro Hotel), directly opposite the Sagrada Familia (Cathedral of Barcelona).

The Case: Markowski-made two-body, solid, polished, curved overhanging lugs, rounded band, snap-on case back. The Dial: Creamy silver with applied yellow gold Breguet numerals, outer minute track, subsidiary dial for the seconds at 6. Gold leaf hands. The Movement: Manual Cal. 12-120, rhodium-plated, fausses cotes decoration, straight line lever escapement, cut bi-metallic balance, blued steel Breguet balance-spring, swan-neck micrometer regulator. Dial, case and movement all signed and with their corresponding serial numbers, all period correct.

The rare Ref. 1596, with its large and elegant yellow gold 36mm case made by the famous Markowski (same casemaker of the Gilbert Albert designed Pateks), features striking overhanging lugs, a dial with applied Breguet numerals, gold leaf hands, a leather strap, and the correct AW buckle for Patek Philippe— already a prized combination for any Patek Philippe collector. What makes this piece truly exceptional, however, is its engraved caseback: “A D. ANTONIO LAMARO LOS INDUSTRIALES DEL HOTEL COLON BARCELONA JUNIO DE 1951” — a dedication from the hotel’s builders to Mr. Lamaro, presented at the hotel’s inauguration.

Lamaro secured one of the most prestigious locations in Barcelona — directly facing the city’s Gothic cathedral made by Gaudí. The Hotel Colón soon became a landmark of elegance and modernity, with panoramic cathedral views from many rooms. Under Lamaro’s ownership, it welcomed cultural icons such as Ernest Hemingway, Tennessee Williams, Jean-Paul Sartre, Sophia Loren, Jane Fonda, and Francis Ford Coppola. Most notably, Joan Miró made the hotel his long-term residence. The presence of such figures reflects Lamaro’s ambition to create more than a hotel — a cultural and artistic haven at the heart of postwar Barcelona. His work exemplified architectural ambition, international vision, and a commitment to excellence — values echoed in the legacy of this extraordinary timepiece.

Patek Philippe Grand Complications Reference 5374/300P-001 Perpetual Calendar Minute Repeater in Solid Platinum with Haute Joaillerie Baguette Diamond Setting, Blue Lacquered Dial with Black-Gradient Rim, Baguette Sapphire Hour Markers and Baguette Diamond Minute Markers, Self-Winding Patek Philippe Caliber R 27 Q Automatic Movement with Cathedral Gongs, Interchangeable Solid and Sapphire Case Backs, Shiny Dusk Blue Alligator Strap with Diamond-Set Platinum Fold-Over Deployant Clasp, 42mm.

The 42mm solid platinum case, curved lugs, and slide-piece minute repeater mechanism are fully integrated within a Haute Joaillerie execution of exceptional refinement. The bezel and flange are set with 120 baguette-cut diamonds totaling approximately 7.10 carats, while the caseband and slide piece are adorned with an additional 86 baguette-cut diamonds totaling approximately 3.50 carats. The dial flange is further accented with 13 baguette-cut sapphires weighing approximately 0.72 carat, creating depth and contrast against the lacquered blue dial. In total, the case and clasp are set with 228 baguette-cut diamonds totaling approximately 11.62 carats. A discreet diamond is set into the platinum case at 6 o’clock, signifying the metal’s exclusivity. The lacquered blue dial with black-gradient rim features baguette sapphire hour markers, baguette diamond minute markers, and white gold leaf-shaped hands with luminous coating. The 18kt gold dial plate supports the perpetual calendar indications, including day, date, month, leap year, and 24-hour display by hands, along with a moon phase aperture. The case measures 12.28mm in thickness and is fitted with interchangeable solid and sapphire crystal case backs.

This Patek Philippe 5374/300P-001 is powered by the self-winding Patek Philippe caliber R 27 Q movement. Constructed with 467 individual components and 39 jewels, the movement integrates a minute repeater with two cathedral gongs and a perpetual calendar complication within a single architecture. The off-center 22K gold guilloched mini-rotor provides automatic winding efficiency while preserving case thickness. The movement includes a Gyromax balance, a Spiromax balance spring, and oscillates at 21,600 semi-oscillations per hour (3 Hz). It carries the Patek Philippe Seal and delivers a power reserve of approximately 38 to 48 hours.

Reference 5374/300P-001 represents the union of two of Patek Philippe’s most revered Grand Complications, perpetual calendar and cathedral minute repeater, executed in platinum and elevated to Haute Joaillerie standards. The luminous geometry created by baguette diamonds and sapphires against the deep blue lacquered dial produces a dimensional effect rarely achieved in high complication watchmaking. This is not simply a timepiece; it is a technical and gem-setting masterpiece reserved for the most discerning collectors.

The striking grey dial contrasts beautifully with the polished case, showcasing applied hour markers and elegant red chronograph accents. The Ref. 425 “Tegolino” is regarded as one of Patek Philippe’s most iconic rectangular watches. Its Italian nickname translates to “little tile,” referencing its slim, elongated, and architectural form. This case design, crafted by the renowned Genevan case maker Markowski, is celebrated for its sculptural lines and symmetry.

The dials of this reference were produced by Stern Frères, and in the finest tradition of early Patek Philippe, feature hand-applied raised enamel signatures. This finishing technique gave the lettering and minute tracks a unique texture, distinguishable even today as a marker of originality. Inside, the manually wound caliber 9-90 provided precise timekeeping in a compact, elegant form, making it one of Patek’s most admired early rectangular calibers.

Often given as gifts, many examples of the Ref. 425 were engraved on the caseback with initials or dedications, further cementing their role as timepieces of both horological and personal importance.

The charm of the Tegolino Ref. 425 lies in its details. The subtle curvature of the dial creates a two-tone effect, while the raised enamel signature is a tactile reminder of Stern Frères’ craftsmanship. This example shows honest signs of wear but retains its character and originality, from the sharp hallmarks on the case to the distinctive long signature with its charming hand-applied enamel finish. It is both a design object and a slice of Patek Philippe’s history.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. This Patek Philippe Ref. 425 is offered in honest condition, representing one of the most iconic rectangular watches of the mid-20th century.


Condition:
Case: The top of the case shows light polishing, while the case sides retain sharp hallmarks; caseback features a hand-engraving with initials, typical of many Ref. 425s gifted during this period.
Crystal: Excellent condition, free of chips or cracks.
Dial: Original dial with light wear, primarily around the sub-seconds register; long raised enamel signature intact with correct early details, including the cut “&” and hand-applied imperfections visible only under magnification.
Hands: Original yellow gold hands in near-perfect condition with minimal wear.
Strap: New black lizard strap in like-new condition.
Buckle: Generic gold-tone buckle in like-new condition.

Pricing

Our prices reflect the competitive value of new and unworn watches, complete with all accessories and a valid manufacturer’s warranty. While we do accept offers to close deals, please understand that our prices are already highly competitive in the market.

Inspection

Before shipping, every watch undergoes a thorough inspection to ensure it matches the description in the listing. We check the timekeeping, case, bracelet, and overall condition, ensuring it’s in perfect order. Each watch is securely packed in its box to ensure safe delivery.

Returns

Buyers have two weeks to inspect their purchase. If the watch is not as described or does not function properly, you may return it for a full refund. Returns will only be accepted if the watch is in the same condition as it was shipped, including all original paperwork and accessories. Watches that have been worn, used, or altered in any way will not be eligible for a return.

Taxes

Sales tax is calculated and added at checkout based on your shipping address, in accordance with state and local laws. Chrono24 collects and remits these taxes directly, and we do not have the ability to waive or alter this amount.

Shipping & Delivery

• United States: All packages are shipped via Overnight shipping and can be delivered to a business address, FedEX office (Hold For Pickup) or a residential address.

• International: We welcome international buyers, and duties/customs fees are the buyer’s responsibility. Certain countries may have shipping restrictions due to insurance limitations, so please contact us to confirm availability.

The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 5980-1A-001 "Blue" from 2015 is a luxurious sports chronograph that embodies the iconic design language of the Nautilus collection. Its 40.50 mm stainless steel case features the signature porthole-inspired shape with integrated lugs, offering both elegance and robustness. The vibrant blue dial is adorned with horizontal embossing and contrasting white subdials, creating a dynamic “panda” aesthetic that enhances legibility and visual appeal. The watch is fitted with a supple leather strap, offering a refined alternative to the traditional metal bracelet, while maintaining the Nautilus’ sporty yet sophisticated character.

Powered by Patek Philippe’s in-house Caliber CH 28-520 C, the 5980-1A-001 delivers exceptional chronograph precision with a 55-hour power reserve. Despite its 120-meter water resistance (not 12,000m, which appears to be a typo), it remains suitable for everyday wear and light aquatic activities. The Nautilus 5980-1A-001 represents the perfect fusion of high-end horology and casual elegance, making it a highly coveted timepiece among collectors and enthusiasts who appreciate Patek Philippe’s legacy of innovation and design excellence.

Patek Philippe is renowned for its watchmaking craftsmanship and heritage, having produced some of the finest mechanical timepieces for over 180 years. The 5711 model embodies that legacy with its precision, attention to detail, and timeless design.

The 5711 is one of the most sought-after models in the Nautilus collection, and its discontinuation has made it even more rare and desirable. This 5711J in yellow gold, had its run from 2006 and was the forerunner imbued with most of the favorite features of the Nautilus DNA., it is very rare to come across compared to the other Nautilus models in other metal colors. The Nautilus is known for its distinctive and iconic design, with its porthole-inspired case, horizontally embossed dial, and open caseback.

The yellow gold version adds a layer of opulence and refinement to this classic look. Very slim case profile with about 8.5mm thickness and makes it perfect to fit under a cuff. The black leather strap adds an extra layer of versatile look to its wearability.

This piece comes a very complete set just as close to as it was originally sold back in 2010. It was recently serviced and package includes,

- 5711J Watch
- Patek Inner Wooden Box and Outer Box
- Brown Patek Suede Cloth for Box
- Certificates and Booklets
- Patek Manuals with their carrying sleeve
- Warranty papers stamped 2010
- Original Receipt from 2010 from the first owner
- Leather Patek Phillipe Travel pouch


This timepiece is in an excellent preowned condition with some very minor signs of wear barely visible to the Naked eye. Our photos have tried to capture high resolution images of the slight signs of wear, no dings, major scratches or bites on the case. Recently serviced and running great.

The 5711J is already a very rare Nautilus model but eve rarer to see is it with Box, Paper, Receipt and this is probably the only one on the market in this condition.


Owning a Patek Philippe signifies success and taste. The brand is considered one of the top-tier luxury watchmakers, and wearing one speaks to a deep appreciation of horological excellence.


If you're looking for a watch that combines prestige, investment potential, and timeless design, the 5711J is a solid choice. Owning one elevates your status as a collector.

Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-001 Grey Dial 2019 Mint Condition Full Set

Available now is the Patek Philippe Nautilus Travel Time Chronograph (Ref. 5990/1A-001), featuring a stainless steel case, grey gradient dial, and integrated bracelet, in mint condition, offered as a complete full set dated 2019.

Included:

Patek Philippe Inner and Outer Box

Certificate of Origin (Dated 2019)

Instruction Manual

Original Stainless Steel Bracelet with Folding Clasp

Condition:

This timepiece is in good condition, showing light handling wear consistent with minimal use. The case and bracelet retain sharp edges and the factory finish. The sapphire crystal and dial are flawless. Macro photos accurately represent the watch’s condition.

About the Watch:

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-001 is one of the most dynamic and functional models in the Nautilus collection, combining dual time zone (Travel Time) functionality with an integrated flyback chronograph — a rare pairing in luxury sports watches.

The grey gradient dial shifts between charcoal and slate depending on the light, with horizontal embossing and perfectly balanced subdials. Two apertures on the dial indicate home and local day/night, while the date display at 12 o’clock syncs with local time.

Powering the watch is the Patek Philippe Caliber CH 28-520 C FUS, an automatic movement featuring a column-wheel flyback chronograph, dual time functionality, and a 45–55-hour power reserve. The movement is visible through the sapphire exhibition caseback, showcasing Patek’s signature finishing and 21k gold rotor.

Key Features:

40.5mm Stainless Steel Case

Grey Gradient Dial with Horizontal Embossing

Dual Time Zone with Day/Night Indicators

Flyback Chronograph with 60-Minute Counter

Patek Philippe Caliber CH 28-520 C FUS Automatic Movement

45–55 Hour Power Reserve

Sapphire Crystal and Display Caseback

Integrated Stainless Steel Bracelet with Folding Clasp

Water Resistance: 120m

The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5990/1A-001 combines sophistication, sportiness, and mechanical excellence — making it one of the most desirable modern Patek references. With its complete 2019 set and stunning grey dial, this example represents both a refined investment and an exceptional daily wearer.

DESCRIPTION & CONDITION:

With an estimated total production of just 100 pieces and approximately 40 appearing in the market - this Reference 3419 is particularly special in that it originally graced the wrist of William Thayer Tutt. Mr.Tutt is generally regarded as the Father of the NCAA Hockey Tournament and served as president of the AHA and the IIHF. Tutt was elected to the United States Hockey Hall of Fame in 1973, to the Hockey Hall of Fame in 1978. He was posthumously inducted into the IIHF Hall of Fame in 2002. Mr. Tutt was also a prominent business person in the state of Colorado.



Please note: the watch lacks a PP crown. There are no tears or kinks to the original mesh bracelet which measures 8 1/2 inches from the buckle spring bar to the last hole. The dial shows aging and a slight patina.

The watch is running accurate to within a minute per day but its service history is unknown.

The Patek Philippe Ref. 5350R “Advanced Research” Annual Calendar is one of the most significant modern references from the brand, limited to just 300 pieces worldwide. Introduced in 2006, it marked Patek’s first public application of silicon technology, featuring the Silinvar escape wheel and Spiromax balance spring—innovations that redefined mechanical precision and durability. Crafted in 18k rose gold with a slate dial, this rare piece represents a pivotal moment in Patek’s pursuit of horological advancement and remains highly coveted for its technical importance, limited production, and exceptional condition.

Moon phase, Date, Month, Annual calendar
Display back, Power Reserve Display, Limited Edition

Patek Philippe 5350R-001 “Advanced Research” Annual Calendar with Moonphase, crafted in 18kt rose gold, and produced in a strictly limited edition of only 300 pieces worldwide. This reference represents one of the most important technological milestones in modern Patek Philippe history. It is not simply a limited edition — it is a laboratory piece released to the public.

The 5350R was introduced under Patek Philippe’s “Advanced Research” program, a series reserved exclusively for groundbreaking technical innovation. The warm rose gold case frames a distinctive silvery-grey anthracite “argenté” dial, lending the watch a contemporary presence while preserving classical Patek proportions. The dial layout features the brand’s celebrated Annual Calendar complication, displaying the day, date, and month, requiring correction only once per year, combined with a beautifully integrated moonphase indication and power reserve display. The balance of information remains clean, legible, and unmistakably Patek.

Powering the watch is the self-winding Patek Philippe Caliber 324 S IRM QA LU, a technically sophisticated automatic movement composed of 355 individual parts. What distinguishes this reference is its revolutionary Spiromax balance spring, unveiled here for the first time in a serially produced Patek Philippe. Through the sapphire exhibition caseback, a magnifying crystal is integrated specifically to highlight this innovation.

The Spiromax balance spring is manufactured from Silinvar, a proprietary high-tech material derived from monocrystalline silicon. Developed in collaboration with a Swiss research institute and select industry partners, Silinvar is anti-magnetic, lightweight, resistant to temperature variation, and immune to corrosion. Patek Philippe engineered a completely new terminal curve geometry for the balance spring, optimizing mass distribution for perfect concentric expansion and contraction across its entire oscillation. The result is improved isochronism, greater precision, and enhanced long-term stability. At the time of release, this was a profound leap forward in regulating organ design.

The movement beats at 28,800 vibrations per hour and is finished to Geneva Seal standards, combining forward-looking material science with traditional haute horlogerie finishing. It is paired with a brown alligator leather strap and secured by an 18kt rose gold deployant buckle, completing a watch that blends classical aesthetics with future-focused engineering.

The 5350R-001 is not simply collectible because it is limited to 300 pieces. It is collectible because it marks a turning point. It is the moment Patek Philippe publicly introduced silicon technology into its regulating organ, laying the foundation for an entirely new era of mechanical advancement. For collectors who value innovation, historical significance, and rarity in equal measure, this is one of the most intellectually important modern Patek Philippe references ever produced.

Only 300 pieces of the 5350R-001 were ever produced, and once released, they were immediately absorbed into serious collections. This is not a commemorative variant or cosmetic limited edition. It is the very first public embodiment of Patek Philippe’s Advanced Research program and the debut of the Spiromax silicon balance spring in a production watch. That alone places it in a category few modern references can claim. As collectors increasingly focus on milestone pieces that changed the technical direction of a manufacture, the 5350R stands apart as a foundational innovation watch. Rare, historically significant, and intellectually important, this is the kind of Patek that does not simply age well, it becomes a benchmark.
